{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015krxb5V1sWmzfmgV3kmNZe","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→## 1.10.0 (November 27, 2024)\n     2→\n     3→NEW FEATURES:\n     4→ - **Ephemeral resources**: Ephemeral resources are read anew during each phase of Terraform evaluation, and cannot be persisted to state storage. Ephemeral resources always produce ephemeral values.\n     5→ - **Ephemeral values**: Input variables and outputs can now be defined as ephemeral. Ephemeral values may only be used in certain contexts in Terraform configuration, and are not persisted to the plan or state files.\n     6→    - **`ephemeralasnull` function**: a function takes a value of any type and returns a similar value of the same type with any ephemeral values replaced with non-ephemeral null values and all non-ephemeral values preserved.\n     7→\n     8→BUG FIXES:\n     9→\n    10→- The `secret_suffix` in the `kubernetes` backend now includes validation to prevent errors when the `secret_suffix` ends with a number ([#35666](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35666)).\n    11→- The error message for an invalid default value for an input variable now indicates when the problem is with a nested value in a complex data type. ([#35465](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35465))\n    12→- Sensitive marks could be incorrectly transferred to nested resource values, causing erroneous changes during a plan ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n    13→- Allow unknown `error_message` values to pass the core validate step, so variable validation can be completed later during plan\n    14→  ([#35537](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35537))\n    15→- Unencoded slashes within GitHub module source refs were being truncated and incorrectly used as subdirectories in the request path ([#35552](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35552))\n    16→- Terraform refresh-only plans with output only changes are now applyable. ([#35812](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35812))\n    17→- Postconditions referencing `self` with many instances could encounter an error during evaluation ([#35895](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35895))\n    18→- The `plantimestamp()` function would return an invalid date during validation ([#35902](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35902))\n    19→- Updates to resources which were forced to use create_before_destroy could lose that flag in the state temporarily and cause cycles if immediately removed from the configuration ([#35966](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35966))\n    20→- backend/cloud: Prefer KV tags, even when tags are defined as set ([#35937](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35937))\n    21→- Simplify config generation (`plan -generate-config-out`) for string attributes that contain primitive types (e.g. numbers or booleans) ([#35984](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35984))\n    22→- config: `issensitive` could incorrectly assert that an unknown value was not sensitive during plan, but later became sensitive during apply, causing failures where changes did not match the planned result ([#36012](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36012))\n    23→- config: The evaluation of conditional expressions and for expression in HCL could lose marks with certain combinations of unknown values ([#36017](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36017))\n    24→\n    25→ENHANCEMENTS:\n    26→\n    27→- The `element` function now accepts negative indices ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n    28→- Import block validation has been improved to provide more useful errors and catch more invalid cases during `terraform validate` ([#35543](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35543))\n    29→- Performance enhancements for resource evaluation, especially when large numbers of resource instances are involved ([#35558](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35558))\n    30→- The `plan`, `apply`, and `refresh` commands now produce a deprecated warning when using the `-state` flag. Instead use the `path` attribute within the `local` backend to modify the state file. ([#35660](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35660))\n    31→- backend/cos: Add new auth for Tencent Cloud backend ([#35888](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35888))\n    32→\n    33→UPGRADE NOTES:\n    34→\n    35→- backend/s3: Removes deprecated attributes for assuming IAM role. Must use the `assume_role` block ([#35721](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35721))\n    36→- backend/s3: The s3 backend now supports S3 native state locking. When used with DynamoDB-based locking, locks will be acquired from both sources. In a future minor release of Terraform the DynamoDB locking mechanism and associated arguments will be deprecated. ([#35661](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35661))\n    37→- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to these references. ([#35850](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35850))\n    38→- config: In order to ensure consistency in results from HCL conditional expressions, marks must be combined from all values within the expression to avoid losing mark information. This typically improves accuracy when validating configuration, but users may see sensitive results where they were lost previously.\n    39→\n    40→## Previous Releases\n    41→\n    42→For information on prior major and minor releases, refer to their changelogs:\n    43→\n    44→- [v1.9](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.9/CHANGELOG.md)\n    45→- [v1.8](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.8/CHANGELOG.md)\n    46→- [v1.7](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.7/CHANGELOG.md)\n    47→- [v1.6](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.6/CHANGELOG.md)\n    48→- [v1.5](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.5/CHANGELOG.md)\n    49→- [v1.4](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.4/CHANGELOG.md)\n    50→- [v1.3](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.3/CHANGELOG.md)\n    51→- [v1.2](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.2/CHANGELOG.md)\n    52→- [v1.1](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.1/CHANGELOG.md)\n    53→- [v1.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.0/CHANGELOG.md)\n    54→- [v0.15](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.15/CHANGELOG.md)\n    55→- [v0.14](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.14/CHANGELOG.md)\n    56→- [v0.13](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.13/CHANGELOG.md)\n    57→- [v0.12](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.12/CHANGELOG.md)\n    58→- [v0.11 and earlier](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.11/CHANGELOG.md)\n    59→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ([#35902](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35902))\n- Updates to resources which were forced to use create_before_destroy could lose that flag in the state temporarily and cause cycles if immediately removed from the configuration ([#35966](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35966))\n- backend/cloud: Prefer KV tags, even when tags are defined as set ([#35937](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35937))\n- Simplify config generation (`plan -generate-config-out`) for string attributes that contain primitive types (e.g. numbers or booleans) ([#35984](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35984))\n- config: `issensitive` could incorrectly assert that an unknown value was not sensitive during plan, but later became sensitive during apply, causing failures where changes did not match the planned result ([#36012](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36012))\n- config: The evaluation of conditional expressions and for expression in HCL could lose marks with certain combinations of unknown values ([#36017](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36017))\n\nENHANCEMENTS:\n\n- The `element` function now accepts negative indices ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n- Import block validation has been improved to provide more useful errors and catch more invalid cases during `terraform validate` ([#35543](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35543))\n- Performance enhancements for resource evaluation, especially when large numbers of resource instances are involved ([#35558](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35558))\n- The `plan`, `apply`, and `refresh` commands now produce a deprecated warning when using the `-state` flag. Instead use the `path` attribute within the `local` backend to modify the state file. ([#35660](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35660))\n- backend/cos: Add new auth for Tencent Cloud backend ([#35888](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35888))\n\nUPGRADE NOTES:\n\n- backend/s3: Removes deprecated attributes for assuming IAM role. Must use the `assume_role` block ([#35721](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35721))\n- backend/s3: The s3 backend now supports S3 native state locking. When used with DynamoDB-based locking, locks will be acquired from both sources. In a future minor release of Terraform the DynamoDB locking mechanism and associated arguments will be deprecated. ([#35661](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35661))\n- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to these references. ([#35850](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35850))\n- config: In order to ensure consistency in results from HCL conditional expressions, marks must be combined from all values within the expression to avoid losing mark information. This typically improves accuracy when validating configuration, but users may see sensitive results where they were lost previously.\n\n## Previous Releases\n\nFor information on prior major and minor releases, refer to their changelogs:\n\n- [v1.9](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.9/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.8](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.8/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.7](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.7/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.6](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.6/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.5](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.5/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.4](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.4/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.3](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.3/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.2](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.2/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.1](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.1/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v1.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.0/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v0.15](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.15/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v0.14](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.14/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v0.13](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.13/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v0.12](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.12/CHANGELOG.md)\n- [v0.11 and earlier](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.11/CHANGELOG.md)\n","numLines":59,"startLine":1,"totalLines":59}}}

map[string]string) map[string]string {\n   858→\tdefined := make(map[string]string)\n   859→\tfor attr, v := range attrs {\n   860→\t\tif val := obj.GetAttr(attr); !val.IsNull() {\n   861→\t\t\tdefined[attr] = v\n   862→\t\t}\n   863→\t}\n   864→\treturn defined\n   865→}\n   866→\n   867→func formatDeprecations(attrs map[string]string) string {\n   868→\tnames := make([]string, 0, len(attrs))\n   869→\tvar maxLen int\n   870→\tfor attr := range attrs {\n   871→\t\tnames = append(names, attr)\n   872→\t\tif l := len(attr); l > maxLen {\n   873→\t\t\tmaxLen = l\n   874→\t\t}\n   875→\t}\n   876→\tsort.Strings(names)\n   877→\n   878→\tvar buf strings.Builder\n   879→\n   880→\tfor _, attr := range names {\n   881→\t\treplacement := attrs[attr]\n   882→\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"  * %-[1]*[2]s -> %[3]s\\n\", maxLen, attr, replacement)\n   883→\t}\n   884→\treturn buf.String()\n   885→}\n   886→\n   887→// Configure uses the provided configuration to set configuration fields\n   782→\treturn obj, diags\n   783→}\n   784→\n   785→// Configure uses the provided configuration to set configuration fields\n   786→// within the backend.\n   787→//\n   788→// The given configuration is assumed to have already been validated\n   789→// against the schema returned by ConfigSchema and passed validation\n   790→// via PrepareConfig.\n   791→func (b *Backend) Configure(obj cty.Value) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   792→\tctx := context.TODO()\n   793→\tlog := logger()\n   794→\tlog = logWithOperation(log, operationBackendConfigure)\n   795→\n   796→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   797→\tif obj.IsNull() {\n   798→\t\treturn diags\n   799→\t}\n   800→\n   801→\tvar region string\n   802→\tif v, ok := stringAttrOk(obj, \"region\"); ok {\n   803→\t\tregion = v\n   804→\t}\n   805→\n   806→\tif region != \"\" && !boolAttr(obj, \"skip_region_validation\") {\n   807→\t\tif err := validation.SupportedRegion(region); err != nil {\n   808→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.AttributeValue(\n   809→\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   810→\t\t\t\t\"Invalid region value\",\n   811→\t\t\t\tfirstToUpper(err.Error()),\n   812→\t\t\t\tcty.GetAttrPath(\"region\"),\n   813→\t\t\t))\n   814→\t\t\treturn diags\n   815→\t\t}\n   816→\t}\n   817→\n   818→\tb.bucketName = stringAttr(obj, \"bucket\")\n   819→\tb.keyName = stringAttr(obj, \"key\")\n   820→\n   821→\tlog = log.With(\n   822→\t\tlogKeyBucket, b.bucketName,\n   823→\t\tlogKeyPath, b.keyName,\n   824→\t)\n   825→\n   826→\tb.acl = stringAttr(obj, \"acl\")\n   827→\tb.workspaceKeyPrefix = stringAttrDefault(obj, \"workspace_key_prefix\", defaultWorkspaceKeyPrefix)\n   828→\tb.serverSideEncryption = boolAttr(obj, \"encrypt\")\n   829→\tb.kmsKeyID = stringAttr(obj, \"kms_key_id\")\n   830→\tb.ddbTable = stringAttr(obj, \"dynamodb_table\")\n   831→\tb.useLockFile = boolAttr(obj, \"use_lockfile\")\n   832→\tb.skipS3Checksum = boolAttr(obj, \"skip_s3_checksum\")\n   833→\n   834→\tif _, ok := stringAttrOk(obj, \"kms_key_id\"); ok {\n   835→\t\tif customerKey := os.Getenv(\"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\"); customerKey != \"\" {\n   836→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(wholeBodyErrDiag(\n   837→\t\t\t\t\"Invalid encryption configuration\",\n   838→\t\t\t\tencryptionKeyConflictEnvVarError,\n   839→\t\t\t))\n   840→\t\t}\n   841→\t}\n   842→\n   843→\tif customerKey, ok := 847→\t\t\t\t\"Invalid sse_customer_key value\",\n   848→\t\t\t\t\"sse_customer_key must be 44 characters in length\",\n   849→\t\t\t\tcty.GetAttrPath(\"sse_customer_key\"),\n   850→\t\t\t))\n   851→\t\t} else {\n   852→\t\t\tvar err error\n   853→\t\t\tif b.customerEncryptionKey, err = base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(customerKey); err != nil {\n   854→\t\t\t\tdiags = %s\", err),\n   858→\t\t\t\t\tcty.GetAttrPath(\"sse_customer_key\"),\n   859→\t\t\t\t))\n   860→\t\t\t}\n   861→\t\t}\n   862→\t} else if customerKey := os.Getenv(\"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\"); AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY value\",\n   867→\t\t\t\t`The environment variable \"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\" must be 44 characters in length`,\n   868→\t\t\t))\n   869→\t\t} else {\n   870→\t\t\tvar err error\n   871→\t\t\tif b.customerEncryptionKey, err = base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(customerKey); err != nil {\n   872→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.WholeContainingBody(\n   873→\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n   874→\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY value\",\n   875→\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(`The environment variable \"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\" must be base64 encoded: %s`, 883→\t\t\"AWS_IAM_ENDPOINT\":      \"AWS_ENDPOINT_URL_IAM\",\n   884→\t\t\"AWS_S3_ENDPOINT\":       \"AWS_ENDPOINT_URL_S3\",\n   885→\t\t\"AWS_STS_ENDPOINT\":      \"AWS_ENDPOINT_URL_STS\",\n   886→\t\t\"AWS_METADATA_URL\":      \"AWS_EC2_METADATA_SERVICE_ENDPOINT\",\n   887→\t}\n   888→\tfor envvar, replacement := range endpointEnvvars {\n   889→\t\tif val := os.Getenv(envvar); val != \"\" {\n   890→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deprecatedEnvVarDiag(envvar, replacement))\n   891→\t\t}\n   892→\t}\n   893→\n   894→\tctx, baselog := baselogging.NewHcLogger(ctx, log)\n   895→\n   896→\tcfg := &awsbase.Config{\n   897→\t\tAccessKey:               stringAttr(obj, \"access_key\"),\n   898→\t\tAPNInfo:                 stdUserAgentProducts(),\n   899→\t\tCallerDocumentationURL: 1153→type argumentRetriever interface {\n  1154→\tRetrieve(diags *tfdiags.Diagnostics) (string, bool)\n  1155→}\n  1156→\n  1157→type attributeRetriever struct {\n  1158→\tobj      cty.Value\n  1159→\tobjPath  cty.Path\n  1160→\tattrPath cty.Path\n  1161→}\n  1162→\n  1163→var _ argumentRetriever = attributeRetriever{}\n  1164→\n  1165→func newAttributeRetriever(obj cty.Value, attrPath cty.Path) attributeRetriever {\n  1166→\treturn attributeRetriever{\n  1167→\t\tobj:      obj,\n  1168→\t\tobjPath:  cty.Path{}, // Assumes that we're working relative to the root object\n  1169→\t\tattrPath: attrPath,\n  1170→\t}\n  1171→}\n  1172→\n  1173→func (r attributeRetriever) Retrieve(diags *tfdiags.Diagnostics) (string, bool) {\n  1174→\tval, err := pathSafeApply(r.attrPath, r.obj)\n  1175→\tif err != nil {\n  1176→\t\t*diags = diags.Append(attributeErrDiag(\n  1177→\t\t\t\"Invalid Path for Schema\",\n  1178→\t\t\t\"The S3 Backend unexpectedly provided a path that does not match the schema. \"+\n  1179→\t\t\t\t\"Please report this to the developers.\\n\\n\"+\n  1180→\t\t\t\t\"Path: \"+pathString(r.attrPath)+\"\\n\\n\"+\n  1181→\t\t\t\t\"Error: \"+err.Error(),\n  1182→\t\t\tr.objPath,\n  1183→\t\t))\n  1184→\t}\n  1185→\treturn stringValueOk(val)\n  1186→}\n  1187→\n  1188→// pathSafeApply applies a `cty.Path` to a `cty.Value`.\n  1189→// Unlike `path.Apply`, it does not return an error if it encounters a Null value\n  1190→func pathSafeApply(path cty.Path, obj cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n  1191→\tif obj == cty.NilVal || obj.IsNull() {\n  1192→\t\treturn obj, nil\n  1193→\t}\n  1194→\tval := obj\n  1195→\tvar err error\n  1196→\tfor _, step := range path {\n  1197→\t\tval, err = step.Apply(val)\n  1198→\t\tif err != nil {\n  1199→\t\t\treturn cty.NilVal, err\n  1200→\t\t}\n  1201→\t\tif val == cty.NilVal || val.IsNull() {\n  1202→\t\t\treturn val, nil\n  1203→\t\t}\n  1204→\t}\n  1205→\treturn val, nil\n  1206→}\n  1207→\n  1208→type envvarRetriever struct {\n  1209→\tname string\n  1210→}\n  1211→\n  1212→var _ argumentRetriever = envvarRetriever{}\n  1213→\n  1214→func newEnvvarRetriever(name string) envvarRetriever {\n  1215→\treturn envvarRetriever{\n  1216→\t\tname: name,\n  1217→\t}\n  1218→}\n  1219→\n  1220→func (r envvarRetriever) Retrieve(_ *tfdiags.Diagnostics) (string, bool) {\n  1221→\tif v := os.Getenv(r.name); v != \"\" {\n  1222→\t\treturn v, true\n  1223→\t}\n  1224→\treturn \"\", false\n  1225→}\n  1226→\n  1227→func retrieveArgument(diags *tfdiags.Diagnostics, retrievers ...argumentRetriever) (string, bool) {\n  1228→\tfor _, retriever := range retrievers {\n  1229→\t\tif v, ok := retriever.Retrieve(diags); ok {\n  1230→\t\t\treturn v, true\n  1231→\t\t}\n  1232→\t}\n  1233→\treturn \"\", false\n  1234→}\n  1235→\n  1236→func stringValue(val cty.Value) string {\n  1237→\tv, _ := stringValueOk(val)\n  1238→\treturn v\n  1239→}\n  1240→\n  1241→func stringValueOk(val cty.Value) (string, bool) {\n  1242→\tif val.IsNull() {\n  1243→\t\treturn \"\", false\n  1244→\t} else {\n  1245→\t\treturn val.AsString(), true\n  1246→\t}\n  1247→}\n  1248→\n  1249→func stringAttr(obj cty.Value, name string) string {\n  1250→\treturn stringValue(obj.GetAttr(name))\n  1251→}\n  1252→\n  1253→func stringAttrOk(obj cty.Value, name string) (string, bool) {\n  1254→\treturn stringValueOk(obj.GetAttr(name))\n  1255→}\n  1256→\n  1257→func stringAttrDefault(obj cty.Value, name, def string) string {\n  1258→\tif v, ok := stringAttrOk(obj, name); !ok {\n  1259→\t\treturn def\n  1260→\t} else {\n  1261→\t\treturn v\n  1262→\t}\n  1263→}\n  1264→\n  1265→func stringSetValueOk(val cty.Value) ([]string, bool) {\n  1266→\tvar list []string\n  1267→\ttyp := val.Type()\n  1268→\tif !typ.IsSetType() {\n  1269→\t\treturn nil, false\n  1270→\t}\n  1271→\terr := gocty.FromCtyValue(val, &list)\n  1272→\tif err != nil {\n  1273→\t\treturn nil, false\n  1274→\t}\n  1275→\treturn list, true\n  1276→}\n  1277→\n  1278→func stringSetAttrOk(obj cty.Value, name string) ([]string, bool) {\n  1279→\treturn stringSetValueOk(obj.GetAttr(name))\n  1280→}\n  1281→\n  1282→// stringSetAttrDefaultEnvVarOk checks for a configured set of strings\n  1283→// in the provided argument name or environment variables. A configured\n  1284→// argument takes precedent over environment variables. An environment\n  1285→// variable is assumed to be as a single item, such as how the singular\n  1286→// AWS_SHARED_CONFIG_FILE variable aligns with the underlying\n  1287→// shared_config_files argument.\n  1288→func stringSetAttrDefaultEnvVarOk(obj cty.Value, name string, envvars ...string) ([]string, bool) {\n  1289→\tif v, ok := stringSetValueOk(obj.GetAttr(name)); !ok {\n  1290→\t\tfor _, envvar := range envvars {\n  1291→\t\t\tif v := os.Getenv(envvar); v != \"\" {\n  1292→\t\t\t\treturn []string{v}, true\n  1293→\t\t\t}\n  1294→\t\t}\n  1295→\t\treturn nil, false\n  1296→\t} else {\n  1297→\t\treturn v, true\n  1298→\t}\n  1299→}\n  1300→\n  1301→func stringMapValueOk(val cty.Value) (map[string]string, bool) {\n  1302→\tvar m map[string]string\n  1303→\terr := gocty.FromCtyValue(val, &m)\n  1304→\tif err != nil {\n  1305→\t\treturn nil, false\n  1306→\t}\n  1307→\treturn m, true\n  1308→}\n  1309→\n  1310→func stringMapAttrOk(obj cty.Value, name string) (map[string]string, bool) {\n  1311→\treturn stringMapValueOk(obj.GetAttr(name))\n  1312→}\n  1313→\n  1314→func boolAttr(obj cty.Value, name string) bool {\n  1315→\tv, _ := boolAttrOk(obj, name)\n  1316→\treturn v\n  1317→}\n  1318→\n  1319→func boolAttrOk(obj cty.Value, name string) (bool, bool) {\n  1320→\tif val := obj.GetAttr(name); val.IsNull() {\n  1321→\t\treturn false, false\n  1322→\t} else {\n  1323→\t\treturn val.True(), true\n  1324→\t}\n  1325→}\n  1326→\n  1327→// boolAttrDefaultEnvVarOk checks for a configured bool argument or a non-empty\n  1328→// value in any of the provided environment variables. If any of the environment\n  1329→// variables are non-empty, to boolean is considered true.\n  1330→func boolAttrDefaultEnvVarOk(obj cty.Value, name string, envvars ...string) (bool, bool) {\n  1331→\tif val := obj.GetAttr(name); val.IsNull() {\n  1332→\t\tfor _, envvar := range envvars {\n  1333→\t\t\tif v := os.Getenv(envvar); v != \"\" {\n  1334→\t\t\t\treturn true, true\n  1335→\t\t\t}\n  1336→\t\t}\n  1337→\t\treturn false, false\n  1338→\t} else {\n  1339→\t\treturn val.True(), true\n  1340→\t}\n  1341→}\n  1342→\n  1343→func intAttr(obj cty.Value, name string) int {\n  1344→\tv, _ := intAttrOk(obj, name)\n  1345→\treturn v\n  1346→}\n  1347→\n  1348→func intAttrOk(obj cty.Value, name string) (int, bool) {\n  1349→\tif val := obj.GetAttr(name); val.IsNull() {\n  1350→\t\treturn 0, false\n  1351→\t} else {\n  1352→\t\tvar v int\n  1353→\t\tif err := gocty.FromCtyValue(val, &v); err != nil {\n  1354→\t\t\treturn 0, false\n  1355→\t\t}\n  1356→\t\treturn v, true\n  1357→\t}\n  1358→}\n  1359→\n  1360→func intAttrDefault(obj cty.Value, name string, def int) int {\n  1361→\tif v, ok := intAttrOk(obj, name); !ok {\n  1362→\t\treturn def\n  1363→\t} else {\n  1364→\t\treturn v\n  1365→\t}\n  1366→}\n  1367→\n  1368→const encryptionKeyConflictEnvVarError = `Only one of \"kms_key_id\" and the environment variable \"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\" can be set.\n  1369→\n  1370→The \"kms_key_id\" is used for encryption with KMS-Managed Keys (SSE-KMS)\n  1371→while \"AWS_SSE_CUSTOMER_KEY\" is used for encryption with customer-managed keys (SSE-C).\n  1372→Please choose one or the other.`\n  1373→\n  1374→func validateNestedAttribute(objSchema schemaAttribute, obj cty.Value, objPath cty.Path, diags *tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n  1375→\tif obj.IsNull() {\n  1376→\t\treturn\n ConfigSchema Changes - Deprecated IAM Attributes REMOVED\n\n**v1.9.0** (lines 216-268) included deprecated IAM role assumption attributes:\n```go\n\"role_arn\": {\n\tType:        cty.String,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"The role to be assumed\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"session_name\": {\n\tType:        cty.String,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"The session name to use when assuming the role.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"external_id\": {\n\tType:        cty.String,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"The external ID to use when assuming the role\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"assume_role_duration_seconds\": {\n\tType:        cty.Number,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"Seconds to restrict the assume role session duration.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"assume_role_policy\": {\n\tType:        cty.String,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"IAM Policy JSON describing further restricting permissions for the IAM Role being assumed.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"assume_role_policy_arns\": {\n\tType:        cty.Set(cty.String),\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of IAM Policies describing further restricting permissions for the IAM Role being assumed.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"assume_role_tags\": {\n\tType:        cty.Map(cty.String),\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"Assume role session tags.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n\"assume_role_transitive_tag_keys\": {\n\tType:        cty.Set(cty.String),\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"Assume role session tag keys to pass to any subsequent sessions.\",\n\tDeprecated:  true,\n},\n```\n\n**v1.10.0** COMPLETELY REMOVED these deprecated attributes from ConfigSchema.\n\n**v1.10.0** ADDED new config option (lines 156-160):\n```go\n\"use_lockfile\": {\n\tType:        cty.Bool,\n\tOptional:    true,\n\tDescription: \"(Experimental) Whether to use a lockfile for locking the state file.\",\n},\n```\n\n### 3. PrepareConfig Method - Deprecation Warnings REMOVED\n\n**v1.9.0** (lines 718-748) included elaborate deprecation mapping and warnings:\n```go\nvar assumeRoleDeprecatedFields = map[string]string{\n\t\"role_arn\":                        \"assume_role.role_arn\",\n\t\"session_name\":                    \"assume_role.session_name\",\n\t\"external_id\":                     \"assume_role.external_id\",\n\t\"assume_role_duration_seconds\":    \"assume_role.duration\",\n\t\"assume_role_policy\":              \"assume_role.policy\",\n\t\"assume_role_policy_arns\":         \"assume_role.policy_arns\",\n\t\"assume_role_tags\":                \"assume_role.tags\",\n\t\"assume_role_transitive_tag_keys\": \"assume_role.transitive_tag_keys\",\n}\n\nif val := obj.GetAttr(\"assume_role\"); !val.IsNull() {\n\tvalidateNestedAttribute(assumeRoleSchema, val, cty.GetAttrPath(\"assume_role\"), &diags)\n\n\tif defined := findDeprecatedFields(obj, assumeRoleDeprecatedFields); len(defined) != 0 {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Error(\n\t\t\t\"Conflicting Parameters\",\n\t\t\t`The following deprecated parameters conflict with the parameter \"assume_role\". Deprecated, moved to `assume_role.tags`\n8. **`assume_role_transitive_tag_keys`** - Deprecated, moved to `assume_role.transitive_tag_keys`\n\n### What Replaced Them\n\nAll the above attributes are now consolidated into a **single `assume_role` nested block** with the following structure:\n- `role_arn` (required)\n- `duration` (optional)\n- `external_id` (optional)\n- `policy` (optional)\n- `policy_arns` (optional, set of strings)\n- `session_name` (optional)\n- `source_identity` (optional)\n- `tags` (optional, map)\n- `transitive_tag_keys` (optional, set of strings)\n\n### Additional Changes\n\n- **Type change**: `cfg.AssumeRole` changed from `*awsbase.AssumeRole` (pointer) to `[]awsbase.AssumeRole` (slice)\n- **New field**: `useLockFile` boolean field added to Backend struct\n- **Removed functions**: `findDeprecatedFields()` and `formatDeprecations()` helper functions no longer exist in v1.10.0\n- **Removed validation**: All deprecation warning/error logic removed from PrepareConfig\n- **Enhanced validation**: Added `validateStringNotEmpty` to `assume_role.role_arn`"}],"totalDurationMs":27023,"totalTokens":59140,"totalToolUseCount":5,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":44595,"cache_read_input_tokens":11605,"output_tokens":2933,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":44595,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BzVfXKBVqTuj3Asvv1nquY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3→\n     4→package addrs\n     5→\n     6→import (\n     7→\t\"fmt\"\n     8→\n     9→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n    10→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    11→)\n    12→\n    13→// MoveEndpoint is to AbsMoveable and ConfigMoveable what Target is to\n    14→// Targetable: a wrapping struct that captures the result of decoding an HCL\n    15→// traversal representing a relative path from the current module to\n    16→// a moveable object.\n    17→//\n    18→// Its name reflects that its primary purpose is for the \"from\" and \"to\"\n    19→// addresses in a \"moved\" statement in the configuration, but it's also\n    20→// valid to use MoveEndpoint for other similar mechanisms that give\n    21→// Terraform hints about historical configuration changes that might\n    22→// prompt creating a different plan than Terraform would by default.\n    23→//\n    24→// To obtain a full address from a MoveEndpoint you must use\n    25→// either the package function UnifyMoveEndpoints (to get an AbsMoveable) or\n    26→// the method ConfigMoveable (to get a ConfigMoveable).\n    27→type MoveEndpoint struct {\n    28→\t// SourceRange is the location of the physical endpoint address\n    29→\t// in configuration, if this MoveEndpoint was decoded from a\n    30→\t// configuration expresson.\n    31→\tSourceRange tfdiags.SourceRange\n    32→\n    33→\t// Internally we (ab)use AbsMoveable as the representation of our\n    34→\t// relative address, even though everywhere else in Terraform\n    35→\t// AbsMoveable always represents a fully-absolute address.\n    36→\t// In practice, due to the implementation of ParseMoveEndpoint,\n    37→\t// this is always either a ModuleInstance or an AbsResourceInstance,\n    38→\t// and we only consider the possibility of interpreting it as\n    39→\t// a AbsModuleCall or an AbsResource in UnifyMoveEndpoints.\n    40→\t// This is intentionally unexported to encapsulate this unusual\n    41→\t// meaning of AbsMoveable.\n    42→\trelSubject AbsMoveable\n    43→}\n    44→\n    45→func (e *MoveEndpoint) ObjectKind() MoveEndpointKind {\n    46→\treturn absMoveableEndpointKind(e.relSubject)\n    47→}\n    48→\n    49→func (e *MoveEndpoint) String() string {\n    50→\t// Our internal pseudo-AbsMoveable representing the relative\n    51→\t// address (either ModuleInstance or AbsResourceInstance) is\n    52→\t// a good enough proxy for the relative move endpoint address\n    53→\t// serialization.\n    54→\treturn e.relSubject.String()\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→func (e *MoveEndpoint) Equal(other *MoveEndpoint) bool {\n    58→\tswitch {\n    59→\tcase (e == nil) != (other == nil):\n    60→\t\treturn false\n    61→\tcase e == nil:\n    62→\t\treturn true\n    63→\tdefault:\n    64→\t\t// Since we only use ModuleInstance and AbsResourceInstance in our\n    65→\t\t// string representation, we have no ambiguity between address types\n    66→\t\t// and can safely just compare the string representations to\n    67→\t\t// compare the relSubject values.\n    68→\t\treturn e.String() == other.String() && e.SourceRange == other.SourceRange\n    69→\t}\n    70→}\n    71→\n    72→// MightUnifyWith returns true if it is possible that a later call to\n    73→// UnifyMoveEndpoints might succeed if given the reciever and the other\n    74→// given endpoint.\n    75→//\n    76→// This is intended for early static validation of obviously-wrong situations,\n    77→// although there are still various semantic errors that this cannot catch.\n    78→func (e *MoveEndpoint) MightUnifyWith(other *MoveEndpoint) bool {\n    79→\t// For our purposes here we'll just do a unify without a base module\n    80→\t// address, because the rules for whether unify can succeed depend\n    81→\t// only on the relative part of the addresses, not on which module\n    82→\t// they were declared in.\n    83→\tfrom, to := UnifyMoveEndpoints(RootModule, e, other)\n    84→\treturn from != nil && to != nil\n    85→}\n    86→\n    87→// ConfigMoveable transforms the reciever into a ConfigMoveable by resolving it\n    88→// relative to the given base module, which should be the module where\n    89→// the MoveEndpoint expression was found.\n    90→//\n    91→// The result is useful for finding the target object in the configuration,\n    92→// but it's not sufficient for fully interpreting a move statement because\n    93→// it lacks the specific module and resource instance keys.\n    94→func (e *MoveEndpoint) ConfigMoveable(baseModule Module) ConfigMoveable {\n    95→\taddr := e.relSubject\n    96→\tswitch addr := addr.(type) {\n    97→\tcase ModuleInstance:\n    98→\t\tret := make(Module, 0, len(baseModule)+len(addr))\n    99→\t\tret = append(ret, baseModule...)\n   100→\t\tret = append(ret, addr.Module()...)\n   101→\t\treturn ret\n   102→\tcase AbsResourceInstance:\n   103→\t\tmoduleAddr := make(Module, 0, len(baseModule)+len(addr.Module))\n   104→\t\tmoduleAddr = append(moduleAddr, baseModule...)\n   105→\t\tmoduleAddr = append(moduleAddr, addr.Module.Module()...)\n   106→\t\treturn ConfigResource{\n   107→\t\t\tModule:   moduleAddr,\n   108→\t\t\tResource: addr.Resource.Resource,\n   109→\t\t}\n   110→\tdefault:\n   111→\t\t// The above should be exhaustive for all of the types\n   112→\t\t// that ParseMoveEndpoint produces as our intermediate\n   113→\t\t// address representation.\n   114→\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported address type %T\", addr))\n   115→\t}\n   116→\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// ParseMoveEndpoint attempts to interpret the given traversal as a\n   120→// \"move endpoint\" address, which is a relative path from the module containing\n   121→// the traversal to a movable object in either the same module or in some\n   122→// child module.\n   123→//\n   124→// This deals only with the syntactic element of a move endpoint expression\n   125→// in configuration. Before the result will be useful you'll need to combine\n   126→// it with the address of the module where it was declared in order to get\n   127→// an absolute address relative to the root module.\n   128→func ParseMoveEndpoint(traversal hcl.Traversal) (*MoveEndpoint, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   129→\tpath, remain, diags := parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal)\n   130→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   131→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   132→\t}\n   133→\n   134→\trng := tfdiags.SourceRangeFromHCL(traversal.SourceRange())\n   135→\n   136→\tif len(remain) == 0 {\n   137→\t\treturn &MoveEndpoint{\n   138→\t\t\trelSubject:  path,\n   139→\t\t\tSourceRange: rng,\n   140→\t\t}, diags\n   141→\t}\n   142→\n   143→\triAddr, moreDiags := parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(path, remain)\n   144→\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   145→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   146→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   147→\t}\n   148→\n   149→\treturn &MoveEndpoint{\n   150→\t\trelSubject:  riAddr,\n   151→\t\tSourceRange: rng,\n   152→\t}, diags\n   153→}\n   154→\n   155→// UnifyMoveEndpoints takes a pair of MoveEndpoint objects representing the\n   156→// \"from\" and \"to\" addresses in a moved block, and returns a pair of\n   157→// MoveEndpointInModule addresses guaranteed to be of the same dynamic type\n   158→// that represent what the two MoveEndpoint addresses refer to.\n   159→//\n   160→// moduleAddr must be the address of the module where the move was declared.\n   161→//\n   162→// This function deals both with the conversion from relative to absolute\n   163→// addresses and with resolving the ambiguity between no-key instance\n   164→// addresses and whole-object addresses, returning the least specific\n   165→// address type possible.\n   166→//\n   167→// Not all combinations of addresses are unifyable: the two addresses must\n   168→// either both include resources or both just be modules. If the two\n   169→// given addresses are incompatible then UnifyMoveEndpoints returns (nil, nil),\n   170→// in which case the caller should typically report an error to the user\n   171→// stating the unification constraints.\n   172→func UnifyMoveEndpoints(moduleAddr Module, relFrom, relTo *MoveEndpoint) (modFrom, modTo *MoveEndpointInModule) {\n   173→\n   174→\t// First we'll make a decision about which address type we're\n   175→\t// ultimately trying to unify to. For our internal purposes\n   176→\t// here we're going to borrow TargetableAddrType just as a\n   177→\t// convenient way to talk about our address types, even though\n   178→\t// targetable address types are not 100% aligned with moveable\n   179→\t// address types.\n   180→\tfromType := relFrom.internalAddrType()\n   181→\ttoType := relTo.internalAddrType()\n   182→\tvar wantType TargetableAddrType\n   183→\n   184→\t// Our goal here is to choose the whole-resource or whole-module-call\n   185→\t// addresses if both agree on it, but to use specific instance addresses\n   186→\t// otherwise. This is a somewhat-arbitrary way to resolve syntactic\n   187→\t// ambiguity between the two situations which allows both for renaming\n   188→\t// whole resources and for switching from a single-instance object to\n   189→\t// a multi-instance object.\n   190→\tswitch {\n   191→\tcase fromType == AbsResourceInstanceAddrType || toType == AbsResourceInstanceAddrType:\n   192→\t\twantType = AbsResourceInstanceAddrType\n   193→\tcase fromType == AbsResourceAddrType || toType == AbsResourceAddrType:\n   194→\t\twantType = AbsResourceAddrType\n   195→\tcase fromType == ModuleInstanceAddrType || toType == ModuleInstanceAddrType:\n   196→\t\twantType = ModuleInstanceAddrType\n   197→\tcase fromType == ModuleAddrType || toType == ModuleAddrType:\n   198→\t\t// NOTE: We're fudging a little here and using\n   199→\t\t// ModuleAddrType to represent AbsModuleCall rather\n   200→\t\t// than Module.\n   201→\t\twantType = ModuleAddrType\n   202→\tdefault:\n   203→\t\tpanic(\"unhandled move address types\")\n   204→\t}\n   205→\n   206→\tmodFrom = relFrom.prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr, wantType)\n   207→\tmodTo = relTo.prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr, wantType)\n   208→\tif modFrom == nil || modTo == nil {\n   209→\t\t// if either of them failed then they both failed, to make the\n   210→\t\t// caller's life a little easier.\n   211→\t\treturn nil, nil\n   212→\t}\n   213→\treturn modFrom, modTo\n   214→}\n   215→\n   216→func (e *MoveEndpoint) prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr Module, wantType TargetableAddrType) *MoveEndpointInModule {\n   217→\t// relAddr can only be either AbsResourceInstance or ModuleInstance, the\n   218→\t// internal intermediate representation produced by ParseMoveEndpoint.\n   219→\trelAddr := e.relSubject\n   220→\n   221→\tswitch relAddr := relAddr.(type) {\n   222→\tcase ModuleInstance:\n   223→\t\tswitch wantType {\n   224→\t\tcase 11→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n    12→)\n    13→\n    14→// MoveEndpoint is to AbsMoveable and ConfigMoveable what Target is to\n    15→// Targetable: a wrapping struct that captures the result of decoding an HCL\n    16→// traversal representing a relative path from the current module to\n    17→// a moveable object.\n    18→//\n    19→// Its name reflects that its primary purpose is for the \"from\" and \"to\"\n    20→// addresses in a \"moved\" statement in the configuration, but it's also\n    21→// valid to use MoveEndpoint for other similar mechanisms that give\n    22→// Terraform hints about historical configuration changes that might\n    23→// prompt creating a different plan than Terraform would by default.\n    24→//\n    25→// To obtain a full 28→type MoveEndpoint struct {\n    29→\t// SourceRange is the location of the physical endpoint address\n    30→\t// in configuration, if this MoveEndpoint was decoded from a\n    31→\t// configuration expresson.\n    32→\tSourceRange tfdiags.SourceRange\n    33→\n    34→\t// Internally we (ab)use AbsMoveable as the representation of our\n    35→\t// relative address, even though everywhere else in Terraform\n    36→\t// AbsMoveable always represents a fully-absolute address.\n    37→\t// In practice, due to the implementation of ParseMoveEndpoint,\n    38→\t// this is always  55→\treturn e.relSubject.String()\n    56→}\n    57→\n    58→func (e *MoveEndpoint) Equal(other *MoveEndpoint) bool {\n    59→\tswitch {\n    60→\tcase (e == nil) != (other == nil):\n UnifyMoveEndpoints might succeed if given the reciever and the other\n    75→// given endpoint.\n    76→//\n    77→// This is intended for early static validation of obviously-wrong situations,\n    78→// although there are still various semantic errors that this cannot catch.\n    79→func (e *MoveEndpoint) MightUnifyWith(other *MoveEndpoint) bool {\n    80→\t// For our purposes here we'll just do a unify without a base module\n    81→\t// address, because the rules for whether unify can succeed depend\n    82→\t// only on the relative part of the addresses, not on which module\n    83→\t// into a ConfigMoveable by resolving it\n    89→// relative to the given base module, which should be the module where\n    90→// the MoveEndpoint expression was found.\n    91→//\n    92→// The result is useful for finding the target object in the configuration,\n    93→// but it's not sufficient for fully interpreting a move statement because\n    94→// it lacks the specific module and resource instance keys.\n    95→func (e *MoveEndpoint) ConfigMoveable(baseModule Module) ConfigMoveable {\n    96→\taddr := e.relSubject\n    97→\tswitch addr := addr.(type) {\n    98→\tcase ModuleInstance:\n    99→\t\tret := make(Module, 0, len(baseModule)+len(addr))\n   100→\t\tret = append(ret, baseModule...)\n   101→\t\tret = append(ret, addr.Module()...)\n   102→\t\treturn ret\n   103→\tcase addr.Module.Module()...)\n   107→\t\treturn ConfigResource{\n   108→\t\t\tModule:   moduleAddr,\n   109→\t\t\tResource: addr.Resource.Resource,\n   110→\t\t}\n   111→\tdefault:\n   112→\t\t// The element of a move endpoint expression\n   126→// in configuration. Before the result will be useful you'll need to combine\n   127→// it with the address of the module where it was declared in order to get\n   128→// an absolute address relative to the root module.\n   129→func ParseMoveEndpoint(traversal hcl.Traversal) (*MoveEndpoint, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   130→\tpath, remain, diags := parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal, false)\n   131→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   132→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   133→\t}\n   134→\n   135→\trng := tfdiags.SourceRangeFromHCL(traversal.SourceRange())\n   136→\n   137→\tif len(remain) == 0 {\n   138→\t\treturn &MoveEndpoint{\n   139→\t\t\trelSubject:  path,\n   140→\t\t\tSourceRange: rng,\n   141→\t\t}, diags\n   142→\t}\n   143→\n   144→\triAddr, moreDiags := parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(path, false, remain)\n   145→\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   146→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   147→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   148→\t}\n   149→\n   150→\treturn &MoveEndpoint{\n   151→\t\trelSubject:  riAddr,\n   152→\t\tSourceRange: rng,\n   153→\t}, diags\n   154→}\n   155→\n   156→// UnifyMoveEndpoints takes a pair of MoveEndpoint objects representing the\n   157→// \"from\" and \"to\" addresses in a moved block, and returns a pair of\n   158→// MoveEndpointInModule addresses guaranteed to be of the same dynamic type\n   159→// that represent what the two MoveEndpoint addresses refer to.\n   160→//\n   161→// moduleAddr must be the address of the module where the move was declared.\n   162→//\n   163→// This function deals both with the conversion from relative to absolute\n   164→// addresses and with resolving the ambiguity between no-key instance\n   165→// addresses and whole-object addresses, returning the least specific\n   166→// address type possible.\n   167→//\n   168→// Not all combinations of addresses are unifyable: the two addresses must\n   169→// either both include resources or both just be modules. If the two\n   170→// given addresses are incompatible then UnifyMoveEndpoints returns (nil, nil),\n   171→// in which case the caller should typically report an error to the user\n   172→// stating the unification constraints.\n   173→func UnifyMoveEndpoints(moduleAddr Module, relFrom, relTo *MoveEndpoint) (modFrom, modTo *MoveEndpointInModule) {\n   174→\n   175→\t// First we'll make a decision about which address type we're\n   176→\t// ultimately trying to unify to. For our internal purposes\n   177→\t// here we're going to borrow TargetableAddrType just as a\n   178→\t// convenient way to talk about our address types, even though\n   179→\t// targetable address types are not 100% aligned with moveable\n   180→\t// address types.\n   181→\tfromType := relFrom.internalAddrType()\n   182→\ttoType := relTo.internalAddrType()\n   183→\tvar wantType TargetableAddrType\n   184→\n   185→\t// Our goal here is to choose the whole-resource or whole-module-call\n   186→\t// addresses if both agree on it, but to use specific instance addresses\n   187→\t// otherwise. This is a somewhat-arbitrary way to resolve syntactic\n   188→\t// ambiguity between the two situations which allows both for renaming\n   189→\t// whole resources and for switching from a single-instance object to\n   190→\t// a multi-instance object.\n   191→\tswitch {\n   192→\tcase fromType == AbsResourceInstanceAddrType || toType == AbsResourceInstanceAddrType:\n   193→\t\twantType = AbsResourceInstanceAddrType\n   194→\tcase fromType == AbsResourceAddrType || toType == AbsResourceAddrType:\n   195→\t\twantType = AbsResourceAddrType\n   196→\tcase fromType == ModuleInstanceAddrType || toType == ModuleInstanceAddrType:\n   197→\t\twantType = ModuleInstanceAddrType\n   198→\tcase fromType == ModuleAddrType || toType == ModuleAddrType:\n   199→\t\t// NOTE: We're fudging a little here and using\n   200→\t\t// ModuleAddrType to represent AbsModuleCall rather\n   201→\t\t// than Module.\n   202→\t\twantType = ModuleAddrType\n   203→\tdefault:\n   204→\t\tpanic(\"unhandled move address types\")\n   205→\t}\n   206→\n   207→\tmodFrom = relFrom.prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr, wantType)\n   208→\tmodTo = relTo.prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr, wantType)\n   209→\tif modFrom == nil || modTo == nil {\n   210→\t\t// if either of them failed then they both failed, to make the\n   211→\t\t// caller's life a little easier.\n   212→\t\treturn nil, nil\n   213→\t}\n   214→\treturn modFrom, modTo\n   215→}\n   216→\n   217→func (e *MoveEndpoint) prepareMoveEndpointInModule(moduleAddr Module, wantType TargetableAddrType) *MoveEndpointInModule {\n   218→\t// relAddr can only be either AbsResourceInstance or ModuleInstance, the\n   219→\t// internal intermediate representation produced by fudging a little here and using\n   235→\t\t\t// ModuleAddrType to represent AbsModuleCall rather\n   236→\t\t\t// than Module.\n   237→\t\t\tcallerAddr, callAddr := relAddr.Call()\n   238→\t\t\tabsCallAddr := AbsModuleCall{\n   239→\t\t\t\tModule: callerAddr,\n   240→\t\t\t\tCall:   callAddr,\n   241→\t\t\t}\n   242→\t\t\treturn &MoveEndpointInModule{\n   243→\t\t\t\tSourceRange: e.SourceRange,\n   244→\t\t\t\tmodule:      moduleAddr,\n   245→\t\t\t\trelSubject:  absCallAddr,\n   246→\t\t\t}\n   247→\t\tdefault:\n   248→\t\t\treturn nil // can't make any other types from a 254→\t\t\t\tSourceRange: e.SourceRange,\n   255→\t\t\t\tmodule:      moduleAddr,\n   256→\t\t\t\trelSubject:  relAddr,\n   257→\t\t\t}\n   258→\t\tcase AbsResourceAddrType:\n   259→\t\t\treturn &MoveEndpointInModule{\n   260→\t\t\t\tSourceRange: e.SourceRange,\n   261→\t\t\t\tmodule:      moduleAddr,\n   262→\t\t\t\trelSubject:  relAddr.ContainingResource(),\n   263→\t\t\t}\n extracted and the source range it was extracted from.\n    28→//\n    29→// If error diagnostics are returned then the Target value is invalid and\n    30→// must not be used.\n    31→func 70→\tdefault:\n    71→\t\tsubject = riAddr\n    72→\t}\n    73→\n    74→\treturn &Target{\n    75→\t\tSubject:     subject,\n    76→\t\tSourceRange: rng,\n    77→\t}, diags\n    78→}\n    79→\n    80→// parseConfigResourceUnderModule attempts to parse the given traversal as the\n    81→// address for a ConfigResource in the context of the given module.\n    82→//\n    83→// Error diagnostics are returned if the resource address contains an instance\n    84→// key.\n    85→func parseConfigResourceUnderModule(moduleAddr Module, remain hcl.Traversal) (ConfigResource, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    86→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    87→\n    88→\tmode := ManagedResourceMode\n    89→\tif remain.RootName() == \"data\" {\n    90→\t\tmode = DataResourceMode\n    91→\t\tremain = remain[1:]\n    92→\t}\n    93→\n    94→\tif len(remain) < 2 {\n    95→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n    96→\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n    97→\t\t\tSummary:  \"Invalid address\",\n 102→\t}\n   103→\n   104→\tvar typeName, name string\n   105→\tswitch tt := remain[0].(type) {\n   106→\tcase hcl.TraverseRoot:\n   107→\t\ttypeName = tt.Name\n   108→\tcase hcl.TraverseAttr:\n  181→\t\t// Starting a resource address with \"resource\" is optional, so we'll\n   182→\t\t// just ignore it.\n   183→\t\tremain = remain[1:]\n   184→\tcase \"count\", \"each\", \"local\", ParseTargetStr is a helper wrapper around ParseTarget that takes a string\n   306→// and parses it with the HCL native syntax traversal parser before\n   307→// interpreting it.\n   308→//\n   309→// This should be used only in specialized situations since it will cause the\n   310→// created references to not have any meaningful source location information.\n   311→// If a target string is coming from a source that should be identified in\n   312→// error messages then the caller should instead parse it directly using a\n   313→// suitable function from the HCL API and pass the traversal itself to\n   314→// ParseTarget.\n   315→//\n   316→// Error diagnostics are returned if either the parsing fails or the analysis\n   317→// of the traversal fails. There is no way for the caller to distinguish the\n   318→// two kinds of diagnostics programmatically. If error diagnostics are returned\n   319→// the returned target may be nil or incomplete.\n   320→func ParseTargetStr(str string) (*Target, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   321→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   322→\n   323→\ttraversal, parseDiags := hclsyntax.ParseTraversalAbs([]byte(str), \"\", hcl.Pos{Line: 1, Column: 1})\n   324→\tdiags = diags.Append(parseDiags)\n   325→\tif parseDiags.HasErrors() {\n   326→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   327→\t}\n   328→\n   329→\ttarget, targetDiags := ParseTarget(traversal)\n   330→\tdiags = diags.Append(targetDiags)\n   331→\treturn target, diags\n   332→}\n   333→\n   334→// ParseAbsResource attempts to interpret the given traversal as an absolute\n   335→// resource address, using the same syntax as expected by ParseTarget.\n   336→//\n   337→// If no error diagnostics are returned, the returned target includes the\n   338→// address that was extracted and the source range it was extracted from.\n   339→//\n   340→// If error diagnostics are returned then the AbsResource value is invalid and\n   341→// must not be used.\n   342→func ParseAbsResource(traversal hcl.Traversal) (AbsResource, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   343→\taddr, diags := ParseTarget(traversal)\n   344→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   345→\t\treturn AbsResource{}, diags\n   346→\t}\n   347→\n   348→\tswitch tt := addr.Subject.(type) {\n   349→\n   350→\tcase AbsResource:\n   351→\t\treturn tt, diags\n   352→\n   353→\tcase AbsResourceInstance: // Catch likely user error with specialized message\n   354→\t\t// Assume that the last element of the traversal must be the index,\n   355→\t\t// since that's required for a valid resource instance address.\n   356→\t\tindexStep := traversal[len(traversal)-1]\n   357→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   358→\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   359→\t\t\tSummary:  \"Invalid address\",\n   360→\t\t\tDetail:   \"A resource address is required. The module path must be followed by a resource specification.\",\n   370→\t\t\tSubject:  traversal.SourceRange().Ptr(),\n   371→\t\t})\n   372→\t\treturn AbsResource{}, diags\n   373→\n   374→\tdefault: // Generic message for other address types\n   375→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   376→\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   377→\t\t\tSummary:  \"Invalid address\",\n   378→\t\t\tDetail:   \"A resource address is required here.\",\n   379→\t\t\tSubject:  traversal.SourceRange().Ptr(),\n   380→\t\t})\n   381→\t\treturn AbsResource{}, diags\n   382→\n   383→\t}\n   384→}\n   385→\n   386→// ParseAbsResourceStr is a helper wrapper around ParseAbsResource that takes a\n   387→// string and parses it with the HCL native syntax traversal parser before\n   388→// interpreting it.\n   389→//\n   390→// Error diagnostics are returned if either the parsing fails or the analysis\n   391→// of the traversal fails. There is no way for the caller to distinguish the\n   392→// two kinds of diagnostics programmatically. If error diagnostics are returned\n   393→// the returned address may be incomplete.\n   394→//\n   395→// Since this function has no context about the source of the given string,\n   396→// any returned diagnostics will not have meaningful source location\n   397→// information.\n   398→func ParseAbsResourceStr(str string) (AbsResource, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   399→\tvar diags 409→\treturn addr, diags\n   410→}\n   411→\n   412→// ParseAbsResourceInstance attempts to interpret the given traversal as an\n   413→// absolute resource instance address, using the same syntax as  452→func validateResourceFromTarget(addr *Target, src *hcl.Range) (AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   453→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   454→\n   455→\tswitch tt := There is no way for the caller to distinguish the\n   490→// two kinds of diagnostics programmatically. If error diagnostics are returned\n   491→// the returned address may be incomplete.\n   492→//\n   493→// Since this function has no context about the source of the given string,\n   494→// any returned diagnostics will not have meaningful source location\n   495→// information.\n   496→func ParseAbsResourceInstanceStr(str string) (AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   497→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   498→\n   499→\ttraversal, parseDiags := hclsyntax.ParseTraversalAbs([]byte(str), \"\", hcl.Pos{Line: 1, Column: 1})\n   500→\tdiags = diags.Append(parseDiags)\n   501→\tif parseDiags.HasErrors() {\n   502→\t\treturn AbsResourceInstance{}, diags\n   503→\t}\n   504→\n   505→\taddr, addrDiags := ParseAbsResourceInstance(traversal)\n   506→\tdiags = diags.Append(addrDiags)\n   507→\treturn addr, diags\n   508→}\n   509→\n   510→// ParsePartialResourceInstanceStr is a helper wrapper around\n   511→// ParsePartialResourceInstance that takes a string and parses it with the HCL\n   512→// native syntax traversal parser before interpreting it.\n   513→//\n   514→// Error diagnostics are returned if either the parsing fails or the analysis\n   515→// of the traversal fails. There is no way for the caller to distinguish the\n   516→// two kinds of diagnostics programmatically. If error diagnostics are returned\n   517→// the returned address may be incomplete.\n   518→//\n   519→// Since this function has no context about the source of the given string,\n   520→// any returned diagnostics will not have meaningful source location\n   521→// information.\n   522→func ParsePartialResourceInstanceStr(str string) (AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   523→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   524→\n   525→\ttraversal, parseDiags := hclsyntax.ParseTraversalPartial([]byte(str), \"\", hcl.Pos{Line: 1, Column: 1})\n   526→\tdiags = diags.Append(parseDiags)\n   527→\tif parseDiags.HasErrors() {\n   528→\t\treturn AbsResourceInstance{}, diags\n   529→\t}\n   530→\n   531→\taddr, addrDiags := ParsePartialResourceInstance(traversal)\n   532→\tdiags = diags.Append(addrDiags)\n   533→\treturn addr, diags\n   534→}\n   535→\n   536→// ModuleAddr returns the module address portion of the subject of\n   537→// the recieving target.\n   538→//\n   539→// Regardless of specific address type, all targets always include\n   540→// a module address. They might also include something in that\n   541→// module, which this method always discards if so.\n   542→func (t *Target) ModuleAddr() ModuleInstance {\n   543→\tswitch addr := t.Subject.(type) {\n   544→\tcase ModuleInstance:\n   545→\t\treturn addr\n   546→\tcase Module:\n   547→\t\t// We assume that a module address is really\n   548→\t\t// referring to a module path containing only\n   549→\t\t// single-instance modules.\n   550→\t\treturn addr.UnkeyedInstanceShim()\n   551→\tcase AbsResourceInstance:\n   552→\t\treturn addr.Module\n   553→\tcase AbsResource:\n   554→\t\treturn addr.Module\n   555→\tdefault:\n   556→\t\t// The above cases should be exhaustive for all\n   557→\t\t// implementations of Targetable.\n   558→\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported target address type %T\", addr))\n   559→\t}\n   560→}\n   561→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NPcYdDzkrbe25amhv8jXKF","type":"tool_result","content":" - **Ephemeral resources**: Ephemeral resources are read anew during each phase of Terraform evaluation, and cannot be persisted to state storage. Ephemeral resources always produce ephemeral values.\n    - **`ephemeralasnull` function**: a function takes a value of any type and returns a similar value of the same type with any ephemeral values replaced with non-ephemeral null values and all non-ephemeral values preserved.\n- Sensitive marks could be incorrectly transferred to nested resource values, causing erroneous changes during a plan ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n- Updates to resources which were forced to use create_before_destroy could lose that flag in the state temporarily and cause cycles if immediately removed from the configuration ([#35966](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35966))\n- Performance enhancements for resource evaluation, especially when large numbers of resource instances are involved ([#35558](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35558))\n- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to these references. ([#35850](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35850))","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_011hfXJTgxP2bLM5iqnscZVi","session_id":"134b5013-2431-4c4f-862b-4e26623f9dfb","uuid":"10e6166c-5937-46f7-beca-bfcc7816b689"}

This change addresses GitHub issue #35850.\n\n### 2. Key Code Changes\n\n#### Function Signature Changes\n\n**v1.9.0:**\n```go\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.9.0/internal/addrs/move_endpoint.go (line 129)\nfunc ParseMoveEndpoint(traversal hcl.Traversal) (*MoveEndpoint, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    path, remain, diags := parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal)\n    ...\n    riAddr, moreDiags := parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(path, remain)\n    ...\n}\n\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.9.0/internal/addrs/module_instance.go (line 83)\nfunc parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal hcl.Traversal) (ModuleInstance, hcl.Traversal, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\n\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.9.0/internal/addrs/parse_target.go (line 154)\nfunc parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(moduleAddr ModuleInstance, remain hcl.Traversal) (AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\n```\n\n**v1.10.0:**\n```go\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/internal/addrs/move_endpoint.go (line 129)\nfunc ParseMoveEndpoint(traversal hcl.Traversal) (*MoveEndpoint, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    path, remain, diags := parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal, false)  // NEW: allowPartial parameter\n    ...\n    riAddr, moreDiags := parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(path, false, remain)  // NEW: allowPartial parameter\n    ...\n}\n\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/internal/addrs/module_instance.go (line 83)\nfunc parseModuleInstancePrefix(traversal hcl.Traversal, allowPartial bool) (ModuleInstance, hcl.Traversal, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\n\n// /workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/internal/addrs/parse_target.go (line 165)\nfunc parseResourceInstanceUnderModule(moduleAddr ModuleInstance, allowPartial bool, remain hcl.Traversal) (AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\n```\n\n#### New Reserved Keywords Validation\n\n**v1.10.0 - `/workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/internal/addrs/parse_target.go` (lines 173-193):**\n\n```go\nmode := ManagedResourceMode\nswitch remain.RootName() {\ncase \"data\":\n    mode = DataResourceMode\n    remain = remain[1:]\ncase \"ephemeral\":\n    mode = EphemeralResourceMode\n    remain = remain[1:]\ncase \"resource\":\n    // Starting a resource address with \"resource\" is optional, so we'll\n    // just ignore it.\n    remain = remain[1:]\ncase \"count\", \"each\", \"local\", \"module\", \"path\", \"self\", \"terraform\", \"var\", \"template\", \"lazy\", \"arg\":\n    // These are all reserved words that are not valid as resource types.\n    diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n        Severity: hcl.DiagError,\n        Summary:  \"Invalid address\",\n        Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The keyword %q is reserved and cannot be used to target a resource address. Reserved Keywords That Now Require \"resource.\" Prefix\n\nThe following reserved keywords now require the `resource.` prefix when used as resource type names in moved blocks:\n\n- `count`\n- `each`\n- `local`\n- `module`\n- `path`\n- `self`\n- `terraform`\n- `var`\n- `template`\n- `lazy`\n- `arg`\n\n### 4. Breaking Change Behavior\n\n**Before v1.10.0 (v1.9.0):** These addresses would have been accepted:\n```hcl\nmoved {\n    from = var.foo        # Would parse as a resource with type \"var\"\n    to   = var.bar\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = local.value\n    to   = local.value2\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = terraform.mode\n    to   = terraform.mode2\n}\n```\n\n**After v1.10.0:** These addresses must now be written as:\n```hcl\nmoved {\n    from = resource.var.foo        # Requires \"resource.\" prefix\n    to   = resource.var.bar\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = resource.local.value\n    to   = resource.local.value2\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = resource.terraform.mode\n    to   = resource.terraform.mode2\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Changelog Reference\n\n**From `/workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/CHANGELOG.md`:**\n```\n- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. \n  Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks \n  and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to \n  these references. (#35850)\n```\n\n### 7. If you are targeting a resource type that uses a reserved keyword, please prefix your address with \"resource.\".`,\n},\n```\n\n### 5. Breaking Change Behavior\n\n**Before v1.10.0 (v1.9.0):** These addresses would have been accepted:\n```hcl\nmoved {\n    from = var.foo        # Would parse as a resource with type \"var\"\n    to   = var.bar\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = local.value\n    to   = local.value2\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = terraform.mode\n    to   = terraform.mode2\n}\n```\n\n**After v1.10.0:** These addresses must now be written as:\n```hcl\nmoved {\n    from = resource.var.foo        # Requires \"resource.\" prefix\n    to   = resource.var.bar\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = resource.local.value\n    to   = resource.local.value2\n}\n\nmoved {\n    from = resource.terraform.mode\n    to   = resource.terraform.mode2\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Changelog Reference\n\n**From `/workspace/terraform-v1.10.0/CHANGELOG.md`:**\n```\n- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. \n  Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks \n  and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to \n  these references.
