The\n    27→// result is a body with those blocks expanded, ready to be evaluated with\n    28→// EvalBlock.\n    29→//\n    30→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n    31→// incomplete or invalid.\n    32→func (s *Scope) ExpandBlock(body hcl.Body, schema *configschema.Block) (hcl.Body, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    33→\tspec := schema.DecoderSpec()\n    34→\n    35→\ttraversals := dynblock.ExpandVariablesHCLDec(body, spec)\n    36→\trefs, diags := langrefs.References(s.ParseRef, traversals)\n    37→\n    38→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.EvalContext(refs)\n    39→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n    40→\n    41→\treturn dynblock.Expand(body, ctx), diags\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→// EvalBlock evaluates the given body using the given block schema and returns\n    45→// a cty object value representing its contents. The type of the result conforms\n    46→// to the implied type of the given schema.\n    47→//\n    48→// This function does not automatically expand \"dynamic\" blocks within the\n    49→// body. If that is desired, first call the ExpandBlock method to obtain\n    50→// an expanded body to pass to this method.\n    51→//\n    52→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n    53→// incomplete or invalid.\n    54→func (s *Scope) EvalBlock(body hcl.Body, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    55→\tspec := schema.DecoderSpec()\n    56→\n    57→\trefs, diags := langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(s.ParseRef, body, schema)\n    58→\n    59→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.EvalContext(refs)\n    60→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n    61→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n    62→\t\t// We'll stop early if we found problems in the references, because\n    63→\t\t// it's likely evaluation will produce redundant copies of the same errors.\n    64→\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(schema.ImpliedType()), diags\n    65→\t}\n    66→\n    67→\t// HACK: In order to remain compatible with some assumptions made in\n    68→\t// Terraform v0.11 and earlier about the approximate equivalence of\n    69→\t// attribute vs. block syntax, we do a just-in-time fixup here to allow\n    70→\t// any attribute in the schema that has a list-of-objects or set-of-objects\n    71→\t// kind to potentially be populated instead by one or more nested blocks\n    72→\t// whose type is the attribute name.\n    73→\tbody = blocktoattr.FixUpBlockAttrs(body, schema)\n    74→\n    75→\tval, evalDiags := hcldec.Decode(body, spec, ctx)\n    76→\tdiags = diags.Append(checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(evalDiags))\n    77→\n    78→\treturn val, diags\n    79→}\n    80→\n    81→// EvalSelfBlock evaluates the given body only within the scope of the provided\n    82→// object and instance key data. References to the object must use self, and the\n    83→// key data will only contain count.index or each.key. The static values for\n    84→// terraform and path will also be available in this context.\n    85→func (s *Scope) EvalSelfBlock(body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block, keyData instances.RepetitionData) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    86→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n    87→\n    88→\tspec := schema.DecoderSpec()\n    89→\n    90→\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n    91→\tvals[\"self\"] = self\n    92→\n    93→\tif !keyData.CountIndex.IsNull() {\n    94→\t\tvals[\"count\"] = cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n    95→\t\t\t\"index\": keyData.CountIndex,\n    96→\t\t})\n    97→\t}\n    98→\tif !keyData.EachKey.IsNull() {\n    99→\t\tvals[\"each\"] = cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n   100→\t\t\t\"key\": keyData.EachKey,\n   101→\t\t})\n   102→\t}\n   103→\n   104→\trefs, refDiags := langrefs.References(s.ParseRef, hcldec.Variables(body, spec))\n   105→\tdiags = diags.Append(refDiags)\n   106→\n   107→\tterraformAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   108→\tpathAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   109→\n   110→\t// We could always load the static values for Path and Terraform values,\n   111→\t// but we want to parse the references so that we can get source ranges for\n   112→\t// user diagnostics.\n   113→\tfor _, ref := range refs {\n   114→\t\t// we already loaded the self value\n   115→\t\tif ref.Subject == addrs.Self {\n   116→\t\t\tcontinue\n   117→\t\t}\n   118→\n   119→\t\tswitch subj := ref.Subject.(type) {\n   120→\t\tcase addrs.PathAttr:\n   121→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetPathAttr(subj, ref.SourceRange))\n   122→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   123→\t\t\tpathAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   124→\n   125→\t\tcase addrs.TerraformAttr:\n   126→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetTerraformAttr(subj, ref.SourceRange))\n   127→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   128→\t\t\tterraformAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   129→\n   130→\t\tcase addrs.CountAttr, addrs.ForEachAttr:\n   131→\t\t\t// each and count have already been handled.\n   132→\n   133→\t\tdefault:\n   134→\t\t\t// This should have been caught in validation, but point the user\n   135→\t\t\t// to the correct location in case something slipped through.\n   136→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   137→\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   138→\t\t\t\tSummary:  `Invalid reference`,\n   139→\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The reference to %q is not valid in this context\", ref.Subject),\n   140→\t\t\t\tSubject:  ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n   141→\t\t\t})\n   142→\t\t}\n   143→\t}\n   144→\n   145→\tvals[\"path\"] = cty.ObjectVal(pathAttrs)\n   146→\tvals[\"terraform\"] = cty.ObjectVal(terraformAttrs)\n   147→\n   148→\tctx := &hcl.EvalContext{\n   149→\t\tVariables: vals,\n   150→\t\tFunctions: s.Functions(),\n   151→\t}\n   152→\n   153→\tval, decDiags := hcldec.Decode(body, schema.DecoderSpec(), ctx)\n   154→\tdiags = diags.Append(checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(decDiags))\n   155→\treturn val, diags\n   156→}\n   157→\n   158→// EvalExpr evaluates a single expression in the receiving context and returns\n   159→// the resulting value. The value will be converted to the given type before\n   160→// it is returned if possible, or else an error diagnostic will be produced\n   161→// describing the conversion error.\n   162→//\n   163→// Pass an expected type of cty.DynamicPseudoType to skip automatic conversion\n   164→// and just obtain the returned value directly.\n   165→//\n   166→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n   167→// incomplete, but will always be of the requested type.\n   168→func (s *Scope) EvalExpr(expr hcl.Expression, wantType cty.Type) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   169→\trefs, diags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(s.ParseRef, expr)\n   170→\n   171→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.EvalContext(refs)\n   172→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n   173→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   174→\t\t// We'll stop early if we found problems in the references, because\n   175→\t\t// it's likely evaluation will produce redundant copies of the same errors.\n   176→\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(wantType), diags\n   177→\t}\n   178→\n   179→\tval, evalDiags := expr.Value(ctx)\n   180→\tdiags = diags.Append(checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(evalDiags))\n   181→\n   182→\tif wantType != cty.DynamicPseudoType {\n   183→\t\tvar convErr error\n   184→\t\tval, convErr = convert.Convert(val, wantType)\n   185→\t\tif convErr != nil {\n   186→\t\t\tval = cty.UnknownVal(wantType)\n   187→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   188→\t\t\t\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\n   189→\t\t\t\tSummary:     \"Incorrect value type\",\n   190→\t\t\t\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expression value: %s.\", tfdiags.FormatError(convErr)),\n   191→\t\t\t\tSubject:     expr.Range().Ptr(),\n   192→\t\t\t\tExpression:  expr,\n   193→\t\t\t\tEvalContext: ctx,\n   194→\t\t\t})\n   195→\t\t}\n   196→\t}\n   197→\n   198→\treturn val, diags\n   199→}\n   200→\n   201→// EvalReference evaluates the given reference in the receiving scope and\n   202→// returns the resulting value. The value will be converted to the given type before\n   203→// it is returned if possible, or else an error diagnostic will be produced\n   204→// describing the conversion error.\n   205→//\n   206→// Pass an expected type of cty.DynamicPseudoType to skip automatic conversion\n   207→// and just obtain the returned value directly.\n   208→//\n   209→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n   210→// incomplete, but will always be of the requested type.\n   211→func (s *Scope) EvalReference(ref *addrs.Reference, wantType cty.Type) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   212→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   213→\n   214→\t// We cheat a bit here and just build an EvalContext for our requested\n   215→\t// reference with the \"self\" address overridden, and then pull the \"self\"\n   216→\t// result out of it to return.\n   217→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.evalContext([]*addrs.Reference{ref}, ref.Subject)\n   218→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n   219→\tval := ctx.Variables[\"self\"]\n   220→\tif val == cty.NilVal {\n   221→\t\tval = cty.DynamicVal\n   222→\t}\n   223→\n   224→\tvar convErr error\n   225→\tval, convErr = convert.Convert(val, wantType)\n   226→\tif convErr != nil {\n   227→\t\tval = cty.UnknownVal(wantType)\n   228→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   229→\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   230→\t\t\tSummary:  \"Incorrect value type\",\n   231→\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expression value: %s.\", tfdiags.FormatError(convErr)),\n   232→\t\t\tSubject:  ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n   233→\t\t})\n   234→\t}\n   235→\n   236→\treturn val, diags\n   237→}\n   238→\n   239→// EvalContext constructs a HCL expression evaluation context whose variable\n   240→// scope contains sufficient values to satisfy the given set of references.\n   241→//\n   242→// Most callers should prefer to use the evaluation helper methods that\n   243→// this type offers, but this is here for less common situations where the\n   244→// caller will handle the evaluation calls itself.\n   245→func (s *Scope) EvalContext(refs []*addrs.Reference) (*hcl.EvalContext, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   246→\treturn s.evalContext(refs, s.SelfAddr)\n   247→}\n   248→\n   249→func (s *Scope) evalContext(refs []*addrs.Reference, selfAddr addrs.Referenceable) (*hcl.EvalContext, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   250→\tif s == nil {\n   251→\t\tpanic(\"attempt to construct EvalContext for nil Scope\")\n   252→\t}\n   253→\n   254→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   255→\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n   256→\tfuncs := s.Functions()\n   257→\tctx := &hcl.EvalContext{\n   258→\t\tVariables: vals,\n   259→\t\tFunctions: funcs,\n   260→\t}\n   261→\n   262→\tif len(refs) == 0 {\n   263→\t\t// Easy path for common case where there are no references at all.\n   264→\t\treturn ctx, diags\n   265→\t}\n   266→\n   267→\t// First we'll do static validation of the references. This catches things\n   268→\t// early that might otherwise not get caught due to unknown values being\n   269→\t// present in the scope during planning.\n   270→\tstaticDiags := s.Data.StaticValidateReferences(refs, selfAddr, s.SourceAddr)\n   271→\tdiags = diags.Append(staticDiags)\n   272→\tif staticDiags.HasErrors() {\n   273→\t\treturn ctx, diags\n   274→\t}\n   275→\n   276→\t// The reference set we are given has not been de-duped, and so there can\n   277→\t// be redundant requests in it for two reasons:\n   278→\t//  - The same item is referenced multiple times\n   279→\t//  - Both an item and that item's container are separately referenced.\n   280→\t// We will still visit every reference here and ask our data source for\n   281→\t// it, since that allows us to gather a full set of any errors and\n   282→\t// warnings, but once we've gathered all the data we'll then skip anything\n   283→\t// that's redundant in the process of populating our values map.\n   284→\tdataResources := map[string]map[string]cty.Value{}\n   285→\tmanagedResources := map[string]map[string]cty.Value{}\n   286→\twholeModules := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   287→\tinputVariables := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   288→\tlocalValues := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   289→\toutputValues := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   290→\tpathAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   291→\tterraformAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   292→\tcountAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   293→\tforEachAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   294→\tcheckBlocks := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   295→\trunBlocks := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   296→\tvar self cty.Value\n   297→\n   298→\tfor _, ref := range refs {\n   299→\t\trng := ref.SourceRange\n   300→\n   301→\t\trawSubj := ref.Subject\n   302→\t\tif rawSubj == addrs.Self {\n   303→\t\t\tif selfAddr == nil {\n   304→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   305→\t\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   306→\t\t\t\t\tSummary:  `Invalid \"self\" reference`,\n   307→\t\t\t\t\t// This detail message mentions some current practice that\n   308→\t\t\t\t\t// this codepath doesn't really \"know about\". If the \"self\"\n   309→\t\t\t\t\t// object starts being supported in more contexts later then\n   310→\t\t\t\t\t// we'll need to adjust this message.\n   311→\t\t\t\t\tDetail:  `The \"self\" object is not available in this context. This object can be used only in resource provisioner, connection, and postcondition blocks.`,\n   312→\t\t\t\t\tSubject: ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n   313→\t\t\t\t})\n   314→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   315→\t\t\t}\n   316→\n   317→\t\t\tif selfAddr == addrs.Self {\n   318→\t\t\t\t// Programming error: the self address cannot alias itself.\n   319→\t\t\t\tpanic(\"scope SelfAddr attempting to alias itself\")\n   320→\t\t\t}\n   321→\n   322→\t\t\t// self can only be used within a resource instance\n   323→\t\t\tsubj := selfAddr.(addrs.ResourceInstance)\n   324→\n   325→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetResource(subj.ContainingResource(), rng))\n   326→\n   327→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   328→\n   329→\t\t\t// Self is an exception in that it must always resolve to a\n   330→\t\t\t// particular instance. We will still insert the full resource into\n   331→\t\t\t// the context below.\n   332→\t\t\tvar hclDiags hcl.Diagnostics\n   333→\t\t\t// We should always have a valid self index by this point, but in\n   334→\t\t\t// the case of an error, self may end up as a cty.DynamicValue.\n   335→\t\t\tswitch k := subj.Key.(type) {\n   336→\t\t\tcase addrs.IntKey:\n   337→\t\t\t\tself, hclDiags = hcl.Index(val, cty.NumberIntVal(int64(k)), ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr())\n   338→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\n   339→\t\t\tcase addrs.StringKey:\n   340→\t\t\t\tself, hclDiags = hcl.Index(val, cty.StringVal(string(k)), ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr())\n   341→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\n   342→\t\t\tdefault:\n   343→\t\t\t\tself = val\n   344→\t\t\t}\n   345→\t\t\tcontinue\n   346→\t\t}\n   347→\n   348→\t\t// This type switch must cover all of the \"Referenceable\" implementations\n   349→\t\t// in package addrs, however we are removing the possibility of\n   350→\t\t// Instances beforehand.\n   351→\t\tswitch addr := rawSubj.(type) {\n   352→\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n   353→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.ContainingResource()\n   354→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCallInstance:\n   355→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.Call\n   356→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCallInstanceOutput:\n   357→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.Call.Call\n   358→\t\t}\n   359→\n   360→\t\tswitch subj := rawSubj.(type) {\n   361→\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n   362→\t\t\tvar into map[string]map[string]cty.Value\n   363→\t\t\tswitch subj.Mode {\n   364→\t\t\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n   365→\t\t\t\tinto = managedResources\n   366→\t\t\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n   367→\t\t\t\tinto = dataResources\n   368→\t\t\tdefault:\n   369→\t\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported ResourceMode %s\", subj.Mode))\n   370→\t\t\t}\n   371→\n   372→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetResource(subj, rng))\n   373→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   374→\n   375→\t\t\tr := subj\n   376→\t\t\tif into[r.Type] == nil {\n   377→\t\t\t\tinto[r.Type] = make(map[string]cty.Value)\n   378→\t\t\t}\n   379→\t\t\tinto[r.Type][r.Name] = val\n   380→\n   381→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCall:\n   382→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetModule(subj, rng))\n   383→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   384→\t\t\twholeModules[subj.Name] = val\n   385→\n   386→\t\tcase addrs.InputVariable:\n   387→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetInputVariable(subj, rng))\n   388→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   389→\t\t\tinputVariables[subj.Name] = val\n   390→\n   391→\t\tcase addrs.LocalValue:\n   392→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetLocalValue(subj, rng))\n   393→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   394→\t\t\tlocalValues[subj.Name] = val\n   395→\n   396→\t\tcase addrs.PathAttr:\n   397→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetPathAttr(subj, rng))\n   398→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   399→\t\t\tpathAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   400→\n   401→\t\tcase addrs.TerraformAttr:\n   402→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetTerraformAttr(subj, rng))\n   403→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   404→\t\t\tterraformAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   405→\n   406→\t\tcase addrs.CountAttr:\n   407→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetCountAttr(subj, rng))\n   408→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   409→\t\t\tcountAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   410→\n   411→\t\tcase addrs.ForEachAttr:\n   412→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetForEachAttr(subj, rng))\n   413→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   414→\t\t\tforEachAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   415→\n   416→\t\tcase addrs.OutputValue:\n   417→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetOutput(subj, rng))\n   418→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   419→\t\t\toutputValues[subj.Name] = val\n   420→\n   421→\t\tcase addrs.Check:\n   422→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetCheckBlock(subj, rng))\n   423→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   424→\t\t\tcheckBlocks[subj.Name] = val\n   425→\n   426→\t\tcase addrs.Run:\n   427→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetRunBlock(subj, rng))\n   428→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   429→\t\t\trunBlocks[subj.Name] = val\n   430→\n   431→\t\tdefault:\n   432→\t\t\t// Should never happen\n   433→\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"Scope.buildEvalContext cannot handle address type %T\", rawSubj))\n   434→\t\t}\n   435→\t}\n   436→\n   437→\t// Managed resources are exposed in two different locations. The primary\n   438→\t// is at the top level where the resource type name is the root of the\n   439→\t// traversal, but we also expose them under \"resource\" as an escaping\n   440→\t// technique if we add a reserved name in a future language edition which\n   441→\t// conflicts with someone's existing provider.\n   442→\tfor k, v := range buildResourceObjects(managedResources) {\n   443→\t\tvals[k] = v\n   444→\t}\n   445→\tvals[\"resource\"] = cty.ObjectVal(buildResourceObjects(managedResources))\n   446→\n   447→\tvals[\"data\"] = cty.ObjectVal(buildResourceObjects(dataResources))\n   448→\tvals[\"module\"] = cty.ObjectVal(wholeModules)\n   449→\tvals[\"var\"] = cty.ObjectVal(inputVariables)\n   450→\tvals[\"local\"] = cty.ObjectVal(localValues)\n   451→\tvals[\"path\"] = cty.ObjectVal(pathAttrs)\n   452→\tvals[\"terraform\"] = cty.ObjectVal(terraformAttrs)\n   453→\tvals[\"count\"] = cty.ObjectVal(countAttrs)\n   454→\tvals[\"each\"] = cty.ObjectVal(forEachAttrs)\n   455→\n   456→\t// Checks, outputs, and run blocks are conditionally included in the\n   457→\t// available scope, so we'll only write out their values if we actually have\n   458→\t// something for them.\n   459→\tif len(checkBlocks) > 0 {\n   460→\t\tvals[\"check\"] = cty.ObjectVal(checkBlocks)\n   461→\t}\n   462→\n   463→\tif len(outputValues) > 0 {\n   464→\t\tvals[\"output\"] = cty.ObjectVal(outputValues)\n   465→\t}\n   466→\n   467→\tif len(runBlocks) > 0 {\n   468→\t\tvals[\"run\"] = cty.ObjectVal(runBlocks)\n   469→\t}\n   470→\n   471→\tif self != cty.NilVal {\n   472→\t\tvals[\"self\"] = self\n   473→\t}\n   474→\n   475→\treturn ctx, diags\n   476→}\n   477→\n   478→func buildResourceObjects(resources map[string]map[string]cty.Value) map[string]cty.Value {\n   479→\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n   480→\tfor typeName, nameVals := range resources {\n   481→\t\tvals[typeName] = cty.ObjectVal(nameVals)\n   482→\t}\n   483→\treturn vals\n   484→}\n   485→\n   486→func normalizeRefValue(val cty.Value, diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   487→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   488→\t\t// If there are errors then we will force an unknown result so that\n   489→\t\t// we can still evaluate and catch type errors but we'll avoid\n   490→\t\t// producing redundant re-statements of the same errors we've already\n   491→\t\t// dealt with here.\n   492→\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(val.Type()), diags\n   493→\t}\n   494→\treturn val, diags\n   495→}\n   496→\n   497→// checkForUnknownFunctionDiags inspects the diagnostics for errors from unknown\n   498→// function calls, and tailors the messages to better suit Terraform. We now\n   499→// have multiple namespaces where functions may be declared, and it's up to the\n   500→// user to have properly configured the module to populate the provider\n   501→// namespace. The generic unknown function diagnostic from hcl does not direct\n   502→// the user on how to remedy the situation in Terraform, and we can give more\n   503→// useful information in a few Terraform specific cases here.\n   504→func checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(diags hcl.Diagnostics) hcl.Diagnostics {\n   505→\tfor _, d := range diags {\n   506→\t\textra, ok := hcl.DiagnosticExtra[hclsyntax.FunctionCallUnknownDiagExtra](d)\n   507→\t\tif !ok {\n   508→\t\t\tcontinue\n   509→\t\t}\n   510→\t\tname := extra.CalledFunctionName()\n   511→\t\tnamespace := extra.CalledFunctionNamespace()\n   512→\t\tnamespaceParts := strings.Split(namespace, \"::\")\n   513→\t\tif len(namespaceParts) < 2 {\n   514→\t\t\t// no namespace (namespace includes ::, so will have at least 2\n   515→\t\t\t// parts), but check if there is a matching name in a provider\n   516→\t\t\t// namspace.\n   517→\t\t\tif d.EvalContext == nil {\n   518→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   519→\t\t\t}\n   520→\n   521→\t\t\tfor funcName := range d.EvalContext.Functions {\n   522→\t\t\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(funcName, \"::\"+name) {\n   523→\t\t\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s Did you mean %q?\", d.Detail, funcName)\n   524→\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   525→\t\t\t\t}\n   526→\t\t\t}\n   527→\t\t\tcontinue\n   528→\t\t}\n   529→\n   530→\t\t// the diagnostic isn't really shared with anything, and copying would\n   531→\t\t// still retain the internal pointers, so we're going to modify the\n   532→\t\t// diagnostic in-place if we want to change the output. Log the original\n   533→\t\t// diagnostic for debugging purposes in case we overwrite something\n   534→\t\t// potentially useful in the future from hcl.\n   535→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] UnknownFunctionCall: %s\", d.Error())\n   536→\t\td.Summary = \"Unknown provider function\"\n   537→\n   538→\t\tif namespaceParts[0] != \"provider\" {\n   539→\t\t\t// help if the user is skipping the provider:: prefix before the\n   540→\t\t\t// provider name.\n   541→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`The function namespace %q is not valid. Provider function calls must use the \"provider::\" namespace prefix.`, namespaceParts[0])\n   542→\t\t\tcontinue\n   543→\t\t}\n   544→\n   545→\t\tif namespaceParts[1] == \"\" {\n   546→\t\t\t// missing provider name entirely\n   547→\t\t\td.Detail = `The function call must include the provider name after the \"provider::\" prefix.`\n   548→\t\t\tcontinue\n   549→\t\t}\n   550→\n   551→\t\tif d.EvalContext == nil {\n   552→\t\t\t// There's no eval context for some reason, so we can't inspect the\n   553→\t\t\t// available functions.\n   554→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`There is no function named \"%s%s\".`, namespace, name)\n   555→\t\t\tcontinue\n   556→\t\t}\n   557→\n   558→\t\totherProviderFuncs := false\n   559→\t\tfor funcName := range d.EvalContext.Functions {\n   560→\t\t\t// there are other functions in this provider namespace, so it must\n   561→\t\t\t// have been included in the configuration, and we can be clear that\n   562→\t\t\t// this a function which the provider does not support.\n   563→\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(funcName, namespace) {\n   564→\t\t\t\totherProviderFuncs = true\n   565→\t\t\t\tbreak\n   566→\t\t\t}\n   567→\t\t}\n   568→\t\tif otherProviderFuncs {\n   569→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(\"The function %q is not available from the provider %q.\", name, namespaceParts[1])\n   570→\t\t\tcontinue\n   571→\t\t}\n   572→\n   573→\t\t// no other functions exist for this provider, so hint that the user may\n   574→\t\t// need to include it in the configuration.\n   575→\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`There is no function named \"%s%s\". Ensure that provider name %q is declared in this module's required_providers block, and that this provider offers a function named %q.`, namespace, name, namespaceParts[1], name)\n   576→\t}\n   577→\n   578→\treturn diags\n   579→}\n   580→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. The\n    27→// result is a body with those blocks expanded, ready to be evaluated with\n    28→// EvalBlock.\n    29→//\n    30→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n    31→// incomplete or invalid.\n    32→func (s *Scope) ExpandBlock(body hcl.Body, schema *configschema.Block) (hcl.Body, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    33→\tspec := schema.DecoderSpec()\n    34→\n    35→\ttraversals := dynblock.ExpandVariablesHCLDec(body, spec)\n    36→\trefs, diags := langrefs.References(s.ParseRef, traversals)\n    37→\n    38→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.EvalContext(refs)\n    39→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n    40→\n    41→\treturn dynblock.Expand(body, ctx), diags\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→// EvalBlock evaluates the given body using the given block schema and returns\n    45→// a cty object value representing its contents. The type of the result conforms\n    46→// to the implied type of the given schema.\n    47→//\n    48→// This function does not automatically expand \"dynamic\" blocks within the\n    49→// body. and returns\n   159→// the resulting value. The value will be converted to the given type before\n   160→// it is returned if possible, or else an error diagnostic will be produced\n   161→// describing the conversion error.\n   162→//\n   163→// Pass an expected type of cty.DynamicPseudoType to skip automatic conversion\n   164→// and just obtain the returned value directly.\n   165→//\n   166→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n   167→// incomplete, but will always be of the requested type.\n   168→func (s *Scope) EvalExpr(expr hcl.Expression, wantType cty.Type) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   169→\trefs, diags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(s.ParseRef, expr)\n   170→\n   171→\tctx, ctxDiags := s.EvalContext(refs)\n   172→\tdiags = diags.Append(ctxDiags)\n   173→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   174→\t\t// We'll stop early if we found problems in the references, because\n   175→\t\t// it's likely evaluation will produce redundant copies of the same errors.\n   176→\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(wantType), diags\n   177→\t}\n   178→\n   179→\tval, evalDiags := expr.Value(ctx)\n   180→\tdiags = diags.Append(checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(evalDiags))\n   181→\n   182→\tif wantType != cty.DynamicPseudoType {\n   183→\t\tvar convErr error\n   184→\t\tval, convErr = convert.Convert(val, wantType)\n   185→\t\tif convErr != nil {\n   186→\t\t\tval = cty.UnknownVal(wantType)\n   187→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   188→\t\t\t\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\n   189→\t\t\t\tSummary:     \"Incorrect value type\",\n   190→\t\t\t\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expression value: %s.\", tfdiags.FormatError(convErr)),\n   191→\t\t\t\tSubject:     expr.Range().Ptr(),\n   192→\t\t\t\tExpression:  expr,\n   193→\t\t\t\tEvalContext: ctx,\n   194→\t\t\t})\n   195→\t\t}\n   196→\t}\n   197→\n   198→\treturn val, diags\n   199→}\n   200→\n   201→// EvalReference evaluates the given reference in the receiving scope and\n   202→// returns the resulting value. The value will be converted to the given type before\n   203→// it is returned if possible, or else an error diagnostic will be produced\n   204→// describing the conversion error.\n   205→//\n   206→// Pass an expected type of cty.DynamicPseudoType to skip automatic conversion\n   207→// and just obtain the returned value directly.\n   208→//\n   209→// If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the result may be\n   210→// incomplete, but will always be of the requested type.\n   211→func (s *Scope) EvalReference(ref *addrs.Reference, wantType cty.Type) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   212→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   213→\n   214→\t// We cheat a bit here and just build an EvalContext for our requested\n   215→\t// reference with the \"self\" ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n   233→\t\t})\n   234→\t}\n   235→\n   236→\treturn val, diags\n   237→}\n   238→\n   239→// EvalContext constructs a HCL expression evaluation context whose variable\n   240→// scope contains sufficient values to satisfy the given set of references.\n   241→//\n   242→// Most callers should prefer to use the evaluation helper methods that\n   243→// this type offers, but this is here for less common situations where the\n   244→// caller will handle the evaluation calls itself.\n   245→func (s *Scope) EvalContext(refs []*addrs.Reference) (*hcl.EvalContext, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   246→\treturn s.evalContext(refs, s.SelfAddr)\n   247→}\n   248→\n   249→func (s *Scope) evalContext(refs []*addrs.Reference, selfAddr addrs.Referenceable) (*hcl.EvalContext, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   250→\tif s == nil {\n   251→\t\tpanic(\"attempt to construct EvalContext for nil Scope\")\n   252→\t}\n   253→\n   254→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   255→\tvals := 0 {\n   263→\t\t// Easy path for common case where there are no references at all.\n   264→\t\treturn ctx, diags\n   265→\t}\n   266→\n   267→\t// First we'll do static validation of the references. This catches things\n   268→\t// early that might otherwise not get caught due to unknown values being\n   269→\t// present in the scope during planning.\n   270→\tstaticDiags := s.Data.StaticValidateReferences(refs, selfAddr, s.SourceAddr)\n   271→\tdiags = diags.Append(staticDiags)\n   272→\tif staticDiags.HasErrors() {\n   273→\t\treturn ctx, diags\n   274→\t}\n   275→\n   276→\t// The reference set we are given has not been de-duped, and so there can\n   277→\t// be redundant requests in it for two reasons:\n   278→\t//  - The same item is referenced multiple times\n   279→\t//  - Both an item and that item's container are separately referenced.\n   280→\t// We will still visit every reference here and ask our data source for\n   281→\t// it, since that allows us to gather a full set of any errors and\n   282→\t// warnings, but once we've gathered all the data we'll then skip anything\n   283→\t// that's redundant in the process of populating our values map.\n   284→\tdataResources := map[string]map[string]cty.Value{}\n   285→\tmanagedResources := map[string]map[string]cty.Value{}\n   286→\tephemeralResources := map[string]map[string]cty.Value{}\n   287→\twholeModules := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   288→\tinputVariables := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   289→\tlocalValues := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   290→\toutputValues := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   291→\tpathAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   292→\tterraformAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   293→\tcountAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   294→\tforEachAttrs := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   295→\tcheckBlocks := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   296→\trunBlocks := map[string]cty.Value{}\n   297→\tvar self cty.Value\n   298→\n   299→\tfor _, ref := range refs {\n   300→\t\trng := ref.SourceRange\n   301→\n   302→\t\trawSubj := ref.Subject\n   303→\t\tif rawSubj == addrs.Self {\n   304→\t\t\tif selfAddr == nil {\n   305→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n   306→\t\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n   307→\t\t\t\t\tSummary:  `Invalid \"self\" reference`,\n   308→\t\t\t\t\t// This detail message mentions some current practice that\n   309→\t\t\t\t\t// this codepath doesn't really \"know about\". If the \"self\"\n   310→\t\t\t\t\t// object starts being supported in more contexts later then\n   311→\t\t\t\t\t// we'll need to adjust this message.\n   312→\t\t\t\t\tDetail:  `The \"self\" object is not available in this context. This object can be used only in resource provisioner, connection, and postcondition blocks.`,\n   313→\t\t\t\t\tSubject: ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n   314→\t\t\t\t})\n   315→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   316→\t\t\t}\n   317→\n   318→\t\t\tif selfAddr == addrs.Self {\n   319→\t\t\t\t// Programming error: the self address cannot alias itself.\n   320→\t\t\t\tpanic(\"scope SelfAddr attempting to alias itself\")\n   321→\t\t\t}\n   322→\n   323→\t\t\t// self can only be used within a resource instance\n   324→\t\t\tsubj := selfAddr.(addrs.ResourceInstance)\n   325→\n   326→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetResource(subj.ContainingResource(), rng))\n   327→\n   328→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   329→\n   330→\t\t\t// Self is an exception in that it must always resolve to a\n   331→\t\t\t// particular instance. We will still insert the full resource into\n   332→\t\t\t// the context below.\n   333→\t\t\tvar hclDiags hcl.Diagnostics\n   334→\t\t\t// We should always have a valid self index by this point, but in\n   335→\t\t\t// the case of an error, self may end up as a cty.DynamicValue.\n   336→\t\t\tswitch k := subj.Key.(type) {\n   337→\t\t\tcase addrs.IntKey:\n   338→\t\t\t\tself, hclDiags = hcl.Index(val, cty.NumberIntVal(int64(k)), ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr())\n   339→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\n   340→\t\t\tcase addrs.StringKey:\n   341→\t\t\t\tself, hclDiags = hcl.Index(val, cty.StringVal(string(k)), ref.SourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr())\n   342→\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hclDiags)\n   343→\t\t\tdefault:\n   344→\t\t\t\tself = val\n   345→\t\t\t}\n   346→\t\t\tcontinue\n   347→\t\t}\n   348→\n   349→\t\t// This type switch must cover all of the \"Referenceable\" implementations\n   350→\t\t// in package addrs, however we are removing the possibility of\n   351→\t\t// Instances beforehand.\n   352→\t\tswitch addr := rawSubj.(type) {\n   353→\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n   354→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.ContainingResource()\n   355→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCallInstance:\n   356→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.Call\n   357→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCallInstanceOutput:\n   358→\t\t\trawSubj = addr.Call.Call\n   359→\t\t}\n   360→\n   361→\t\tswitch subj := rawSubj.(type) {\n   362→\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n   363→\t\t\tvar into map[string]map[string]cty.Value\n   364→\t\t\tswitch subj.Mode {\n   365→\t\t\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n   366→\t\t\t\tinto = managedResources\n   367→\t\t\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n   368→\t\t\t\tinto = dataResources\n   369→\t\t\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n   370→\t\t\t\tinto = ephemeralResources\n   371→\t\t\tdefault:\n   372→\t\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported ResourceMode %s\", subj.Mode))\n   373→\t\t\t}\n   374→\n   375→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetResource(subj, rng))\n   376→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   377→\n   378→\t\t\tr := subj\n   379→\t\t\tif into[r.Type] == nil {\n   380→\t\t\t\tinto[r.Type] = make(map[string]cty.Value)\n   381→\t\t\t}\n   382→\t\t\tinto[r.Type][r.Name] = val\n   383→\n   384→\t\tcase addrs.ModuleCall:\n   385→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetModule(subj, rng))\n   386→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   387→\t\t\twholeModules[subj.Name] = val\n   388→\n   389→\t\tcase addrs.InputVariable:\n   390→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetInputVariable(subj, rng))\n   391→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   392→\t\t\tinputVariables[subj.Name] = val\n   393→\n   394→\t\tcase addrs.LocalValue:\n   395→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetLocalValue(subj, rng))\n   396→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   397→\t\t\tlocalValues[subj.Name] = val\n   398→\n   399→\t\tcase addrs.PathAttr:\n   400→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetPathAttr(subj, rng))\n   401→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   402→\t\t\tpathAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   403→\n   404→\t\tcase addrs.TerraformAttr:\n   405→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetTerraformAttr(subj, rng))\n   406→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   407→\t\t\tterraformAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   408→\n   409→\t\tcase addrs.CountAttr:\n   410→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetCountAttr(subj, rng))\n   411→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   412→\t\t\tcountAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   413→\n   414→\t\tcase addrs.ForEachAttr:\n   415→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetForEachAttr(subj, rng))\n   416→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   417→\t\t\tforEachAttrs[subj.Name] = val\n   418→\n   419→\t\tcase addrs.OutputValue:\n   420→\t\t\tval, valDiags := 430→\t\t\tval, valDiags := normalizeRefValue(s.Data.GetRunBlock(subj, rng))\n   431→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(valDiags)\n   432→\t\t\trunBlocks[subj.Name] = val\n   433→\n   434→\t\tdefault:\n   435→\t\t\t// Should never happen\n   436→\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"Scope.buildEvalContext cannot handle address type %T\", rawSubj))\n   437→\t\t}\n   438→\t}\n   439→\n   440→\t// Managed resources are exposed in two different locations. The primary\n   441→\t// is at the top level where the resource type name is the root of the\n   442→\t// traversal, but we also expose them under \"resource\" as an escaping\n   443→\t// technique if we add a reserved name in a future language edition which\n   444→\t// conflicts with someone's existing provider.\n   445→\tfor k, v := range buildResourceObjects(managedResources) {\n   446→\t\tvals[k] = v\n   447→\t}\n   448→\tvals[\"resource\"] = cty.ObjectVal(buildResourceObjects(managedResources))\n   449→\tvals[\"ephemeral\"] = cty.ObjectVal(buildResourceObjects(ephemeralResources))\n   450→\tvals[\"data\"] = cty.ObjectVal(buildResourceObjects(dataResources))\n   451→\tvals[\"module\"] = cty.ObjectVal(wholeModules)\n   452→\tvals[\"var\"] = cty.ObjectVal(inputVariables)\n   453→\tvals[\"local\"] = cty.ObjectVal(localValues)\n   454→\tvals[\"path\"] = cty.ObjectVal(pathAttrs)\n   455→\tvals[\"terraform\"] = cty.ObjectVal(terraformAttrs)\n   456→\tvals[\"count\"] = cty.ObjectVal(countAttrs)\n   457→\tvals[\"each\"] = cty.ObjectVal(forEachAttrs)\n   458→\n   459→\t// Checks, outputs, and run blocks are conditionally included in the\n   460→\t// available scope, so we'll only write out their values if we actually have\n   461→\t// something for them.\n   462→\tif len(checkBlocks) > 0 {\n   463→\t\tvals[\"check\"] = cty.ObjectVal(checkBlocks)\n   464→\t}\n   465→\n   466→\tif len(outputValues) > 0 {\n   467→\t\tvals[\"output\"] = cty.ObjectVal(outputValues)\n   468→\t}\n   469→\n   470→\tif len(runBlocks) > 0 {\n   471→\t\tvals[\"run\"] = cty.ObjectVal(runBlocks)\n   472→\t}\n   473→\n   474→\tif self != cty.NilVal {\n   475→\t\tvals[\"self\"] = self\n   476→\t}\n   477→\n   478→\treturn ctx, diags\n   479→}\n   480→\n   481→func buildResourceObjects(resources map[string]map[string]cty.Value) map[string]cty.Value {\n   482→\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n   483→\tfor typeName, nameVals := range resources {\n   484→\t\tvals[typeName] = cty.ObjectVal(nameVals)\n   485→\t}\n   486→\treturn vals\n   487→}\n   488→\n   489→func normalizeRefValue(val cty.Value, diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   490→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   491→\t\t// If there are errors then we will force an unknown result so that\n   492→\t\t// we can still evaluate and catch type errors but we'll avoid\n   493→\t\t// producing redundant re-statements of the same errors we've already\n   494→\t\t// dealt with here.\n   495→\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(val.Type()), diags\n   496→\t}\n   497→\treturn val, diags\n   498→}\n   499→\n   500→// checkForUnknownFunctionDiags inspects the diagnostics for errors from unknown\n   501→// function calls, and tailors the messages to better suit Terraform. We now\n   502→// have multiple namespaces where functions may be declared, and it's up to the\n   503→// user to have properly configured the module to populate the provider\n   504→// namespace. The generic unknown function diagnostic from hcl does not direct\n   505→// the user on how to remedy the situation in Terraform, and we can give more\n   506→// useful information in a few Terraform specific cases here.\n   507→func checkForUnknownFunctionDiags(diags hcl.Diagnostics) hcl.Diagnostics {\n   508→\tfor _, d := range diags {\n   509→\t\textra, ok := hcl.DiagnosticExtra[hclsyntax.FunctionCallUnknownDiagExtra](d)\n   510→\t\tif !ok {\n   511→\t\t\tcontinue\n   512→\t\t}\n   513→\t\tname := extra.CalledFunctionName()\n   514→\t\tnamespace := extra.CalledFunctionNamespace()\n   515→\t\tnamespaceParts := strings.Split(namespace, \"::\")\n   516→\t\tif len(namespaceParts) < 2 {\n   517→\t\t\t// no namespace (namespace includes ::, so will have at least 2\n   518→\t\t\t// parts), but check if there is a matching name in a provider\n   519→\t\t\t// namspace.\n   520→\t\t\tif d.EvalContext == nil {\n   521→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   522→\t\t\t}\n   523→\n   524→\t\t\tfor funcName := range d.EvalContext.Functions {\n   525→\t\t\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(funcName, \"::\"+name) {\n   526→\t\t\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s Did you mean %q?\", d.Detail, funcName)\n   527→\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   528→\t\t\t\t}\n   529→\t\t\t}\n   530→\t\t\tcontinue\n   531→\t\t}\n   532→\n   533→\t\t// the diagnostic isn't really shared with anything, and copying would\n   534→\t\t// still retain the internal pointers, so we're going to modify the\n   535→\t\t// diagnostic in-place if we want to change the output. Log the original\n   536→\t\t// diagnostic for debugging purposes in case we overwrite something\n   537→\t\t// potentially useful in the future from hcl.\n   538→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] UnknownFunctionCall: %s\", d.Error())\n   539→\t\td.Summary = \"Unknown provider function\"\n   540→\n   541→\t\tif namespaceParts[0] != \"provider\" {\n   542→\t\t\t// help if the user is skipping the provider:: prefix before the\n   543→\t\t\t// provider name.\n   544→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`The function namespace %q is not valid. Provider function calls must use the \"provider::\" namespace prefix.`, namespaceParts[0])\n   545→\t\t\tcontinue\n   546→\t\t}\n   547→\n   548→\t\tif namespaceParts[1] == \"\" {\n   549→\t\t\t// missing provider name entirely\n   550→\t\t\td.Detail = `The function call must include the provider name after the \"provider::\" prefix.`\n   551→\t\t\tcontinue\n   552→\t\t}\n   553→\n   554→\t\tif d.EvalContext == nil {\n   555→\t\t\t// There's no eval context for some reason, so we can't inspect the\n   556→\t\t\t// available functions.\n   557→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`There is no function named \"%s%s\".`, namespace, name)\n   558→\t\t\tcontinue\n   559→\t\t}\n   560→\n   561→\t\totherProviderFuncs := false\n   562→\t\tfor funcName := range d.EvalContext.Functions {\n   563→\t\t\t// there are other functions in this provider namespace, so it must\n   564→\t\t\t// have been included in the configuration, and we can be clear that\n   565→\t\t\t// this a function which the provider does not support.\n   566→\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(funcName, namespace) {\n   567→\t\t\t\totherProviderFuncs = true\n   568→\t\t\t\tbreak\n   569→\t\t\t}\n   570→\t\t}\n   571→\t\tif otherProviderFuncs {\n   572→\t\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(\"The function %q is not available from the provider %q.\", name, namespaceParts[1])\n   573→\t\t\tcontinue\n   574→\t\t}\n   575→\n   576→\t\t// no other functions exist for this provider, so hint that the user may\n   577→\t\t// need to include it in the configuration.\n   578→\t\td.Detail = fmt.Sprintf(`There is no function named \"%s%s\". Ensure that provider name %q is declared in this module's required_providers block, and that this provider offers a function named %q.`, namespace, name, namespaceParts[1], name)\n   579→\t}\n   580→\n   581→\treturn diags\n   582→}\n   583→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. (This situation occurring\n    51→\t\t\t// would imply an inconsistency/bug elsewhere, so we're just\n    52→\t\t\t// being robust about it here.)\n    53→\t\t\tcty.NumberIntVal(1).Mark(\"bloop\"),\n    54→\t\t\t``,\n    55→\t\t},\n    56→\t\t{\n    57→\t\t\t// A value deep already marked is allowed and stays marked,\n    58→\t\t\t// _and_ we'll also mark the outer collection as sensitive.\n    59→\t\t\tcty.ListVal([]cty.Value{cty.NumberIntVal(1).Mark(marks.Sensitive)}),\n    60→\t\t\t``,\n    61→\t\t},\n    62→\t}\n    63→\n    64→\tfor _, test := range tests {\n    65→\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"sensitive(%#v)\", test.Input), func(t *testing.T) {\n    66→\t\t\tgot, err := Sensitive(test.Input)\n    67→\n    68→\t\t\tif test.WantErr != \"\" {\n    69→\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n    70→\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"succeeded; want error\")\n    71→\t\t\t\t}\n    72→\t\t\t\tif got, want := err.Error(), test.WantErr; got != want {\n    73→\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong error\\ngot:  %s\\nwant: %s\", got, want)\n    74→\t\t\t\t}\n    75→\t\t\t\treturn\n    76→\t\t\t} else if err != nil {\n    77→\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n    78→\t\t\t}\n    79→\n    80→\t\t\tif !got.HasMark(marks.Sensitive) {\n    81→\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"result is not marked sensitive\")\n    82→\t\t\t}\n    83→\n    84→\t\t\tgotRaw, gotMarks := got.Unmark()\n    85→\t\t\tif len(gotMarks) != 1 {\n    86→\t\t\t\t// We're only expecting to have the \"sensitive\" mark we checked\n    87→\t\t\t\t// above. Any others are an error, even if they happen to\n    88→\t\t\t\t// appear alongside \"sensitive\". (We might change this rule\n    89→\t\t\t\t// if someday we decide to use marks for some additional\n    90→\t\t\t\t// unrelated thing in Terraform, but currently we assume that\n    91→\t\t\t\t// {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TVDA5qmpJSgFyDWc5hEwPa","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→## 1.10.0 (November 27, 2024)\n     2→\n     3→NEW FEATURES:\n     4→ - **Ephemeral resources**: Ephemeral resources are read anew during each phase of Terraform evaluation, and cannot be persisted to state storage. Ephemeral resources always produce ephemeral values.\n     5→ - **Ephemeral values**: Input variables and outputs can now be defined as ephemeral. Ephemeral values may only be used in certain contexts in Terraform configuration, and are not persisted to the plan or state files.\n     6→    - **`ephemeralasnull` function**: a function takes a value of any type and returns a similar value of the same type with any ephemeral values replaced with non-ephemeral null values and all non-ephemeral values preserved.\n     7→\n     8→BUG FIXES:\n     9→\n    10→- The `secret_suffix` in the `kubernetes` backend now includes validation to prevent errors when the `secret_suffix` ends with a number ([#35666](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35666)).\n    11→- The error message for an invalid default value for an input variable now indicates when the problem is with a nested value in a complex data type. ([#35465](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35465))\n    12→- Sensitive marks could be incorrectly transferred to nested resource values, causing erroneous changes during a plan ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n    13→- Allow unknown `error_message` values to pass the core validate step, so variable validation can be completed later during plan\n    14→  ([#35537](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35537))\n    15→- Unencoded slashes within GitHub module source refs were being truncated and incorrectly used as subdirectories in the request path ([#35552](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35552))\n    16→- Terraform refresh-only plans with output only changes are now applyable. ([#35812](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35812))\n    17→- Postconditions referencing `self` with many instances could encounter an error during evaluation ([#35895](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35895))\n    18→- The `plantimestamp()` function would return an invalid date during validation ([#35902](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35902))\n    19→- Updates to resources which were forced to use create_before_destroy could lose that flag in the state temporarily and cause cycles if immediately removed from the configuration ([#35966](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35966))\n    20→- backend/cloud: Prefer KV tags, even when tags are defined as set ([#35937](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35937))\n    21→- Simplify config generation (`plan -generate-config-out`) for string attributes that contain primitive types (e.g. numbers or booleans) ([#35984](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35984))\n    22→- config: `issensitive` could incorrectly assert that an unknown value was not sensitive during plan, but later became sensitive during apply, causing failures where changes did not match the planned result ([#36012](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36012))\n    23→- config: The evaluation of conditional expressions and for expression in HCL could lose marks with certain combinations of unknown values ([#36017](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36017))\n    24→\n    25→ENHANCEMENTS:\n    26→\n    27→- The `element` function now accepts negative indices ([#35501](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35501))\n    28→- Import block validation has been improved to provide more useful errors and catch more invalid cases during `terraform validate` ([#35543](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35543))\n    29→- Performance enhancements for resource evaluation, especially when large numbers of resource instances are involved ([#35558](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35558))\n    30→- The `plan`, `apply`, and `refresh` commands now produce a deprecated warning when using the `-state` flag. Instead use the `path` attribute within the `local` backend to modify the state file. ([#35660](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35660))\n    31→- backend/cos: Add new auth for Tencent Cloud backend ([#35888](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/pull/35888))\n    32→\n    33→UPGRADE NOTES:\n    34→\n    35→- backend/s3: Removes deprecated attributes for assuming IAM role. Must use the `assume_role` block ([#35721](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35721))\n    36→- backend/s3: The s3 backend now supports S3 native state locking. When used with DynamoDB-based locking, locks will be acquired from both sources. In a future minor release of Terraform the DynamoDB locking mechanism and associated arguments will be deprecated. ([#35661](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35661))\n    37→- `moved`: Moved blocks now respect reserved keywords when parsing resource addresses. Configurations that reference resources with type names that match top level blocks and keywords from `moved` blocks will need to prepend the `resource.` identifier to these references. ([#35850](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/35850))\n    38→- config: In order to ensure consistency in results from HCL conditional expressions, marks must be combined from all values within the expression to avoid losing mark information. This typically improves accuracy when validating configuration, but users may see sensitive results where they were lost previously.\n    39→\n    40→## Previous Releases\n    41→\n    42→For information on prior major and minor releases, refer to their changelogs:\n    43→\n    44→- [v1.9](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.9/CHANGELOG.md)\n    45→- [v1.8](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.8/CHANGELOG.md)\n    46→- [v1.7](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.7/CHANGELOG.md)\n    47→- [v1.6](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.6/CHANGELOG.md)\n    48→- [v1.5](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.5/CHANGELOG.md)\n    49→- [v1.4](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.4/CHANGELOG.md)\n    50→- [v1.3](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.3/CHANGELOG.md)\n    51→- [v1.2](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.2/CHANGELOG.md)\n    52→- [v1.1](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.1/CHANGELOG.md)\n    53→- [v1.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.0/CHANGELOG.md)\n    54→- [v0.15](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.15/CHANGELOG.md)\n    55→- [v0.14](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.14/CHANGELOG.md)\n    56→- [v0.13](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.13/CHANGELOG.md)\n    57→- [v0.12](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.12/CHANGELOG.md)\n    58→- [v0.11 and earlier](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.11/CHANGELOG.md)\n    59→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. In particular, this means that you can pass\n    21→// cty.List(cty.DynamicPseudoType) to mean \"list of any single type\", which\n    22→// will then cause cty to attempt to unify all of the element types when given\n    23→// a tuple.\n    24→func MakeToFunc(wantTy cty.Type) function.Function {\n    25→\treturn function.New(&function.Spec{\n    26→\t\tParams: []function.Parameter{\n    27→\t\t\t{\n    28→\t\t\t\tName: \"v\",\n    29→\t\t\t\t// We use DynamicPseudoType rather than wantTy here so that\n    30→\t\t\t\t// all values will pass through the function API verbatim and\n    31→\t\t\t\t// we can handle the conversion logic within the Type and\n    32→\t\t\t\t// Impl functions. This allows us to customize the error\n    33→\t\t\t\t// messages to be more appropriate for an explicit type\n    34→\t\t\t\t// conversion, whereas the cty function system produces\n    35→\t\t\t\t// messages aimed at _implicit_ type conversions.\n    36→\t\t\t\tType:             cty.DynamicPseudoType,\n    37→\t\t\t\tAllowNull:        true,\n    38→\t\t\t\tAllowMarked:      true,\n    39→\t\t\t\tAllowDynamicType: true,\n    40→\t\t\t\tAllowUnknown:     true,\n    41→\t\t\t},\n    42→\t\t},\n    43→\t\tType: func(args []cty.Value) (cty.Type, error) {\n    44→\t\t\tgotTy := args[0].Type()\n    45→\t\t\tif gotTy.Equals(wantTy) {\n    46→\t\t\t\treturn wantTy, nil\n    47→\t\t\t}\n    48→\t\t\tconv := convert.GetConversionUnsafe(args[0].Type(), wantTy)\n    49→\t\t\tif conv == nil {\n    50→\t\t\t\t// We'll use some specialized errors for some trickier cases,\n    51→\t\t\t\t// but most we can handle in a simple way.\n    52→\t\t\t\tswitch {\n    53→\t\t\t\tcase gotTy.IsTupleType() && wantTy.IsTupleType():\n    54→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.NilType, function.NewArgErrorf(0, \"incompatible tuple type for conversion: %s\", convert.MismatchMessage(gotTy, wantTy))\n    55→\t\t\t\tcase gotTy.IsObjectType() && wantTy.IsObjectType():\n    56→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.NilType, function.NewArgErrorf(0, \"incompatible object type for conversion: %s\", convert.MismatchMessage(gotTy, wantTy))\n    57→\t\t\t\tdefault:\n    58→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.NilType, function.NewArgErrorf(0, \"cannot convert %s to %s\", gotTy.FriendlyName(), wantTy.FriendlyNameForConstraint())\n    59→\t\t\t\t}\n    60→\t\t\t}\n    61→\t\t\t// If a conversion is available then everything is fine.\n    62→\t\t\treturn wantTy, nil\n    63→\t\t},\n    64→\t\tImpl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (cty.Value, error) {\n    65→\t\t\tif !args[0].IsKnown() {\n    66→\t\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(retType).WithSameMarks(args[0]), nil\n    67→\t\t\t}\n    68→\n    69→\t\t\tret, err := convert.Convert(args[0], retType)\n    70→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    71→\t\t\t\tval, _ := args[0].UnmarkDeep()\n    72→\t\t\t\t// Because we used GetConversionUnsafe above, conversion can\n    73→\t\t\t\t// still potentially fail in here. For example, if the user\n    74→\t\t\t\t// asks to convert the string \"a\" to bool then we'll\n    75→\t\t\t\t// optimistically permit it during type checking but fail here\n    76→\t\t\t\t// once we note that the value isn't either \"true\" or \"false\".\n    77→\t\t\t\tgotTy := val.Type()\n    78→\t\t\t\tswitch {\n    79→\t\t\t\tcase marks.Contains(args[0], marks.Sensitive):\n    80→\t\t\t\t\t// Generic message so we won't inadvertently disclose\n    81→\t\t\t\t\t// information about sensitive values.\n    82→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.NilVal, function.NewArgErrorf(0, \"cannot convert this sensitive %s to %s\", gotTy.FriendlyName(), wantTy.FriendlyNameForConstraint())\n    83→\n    84→\t\t\t\tcase gotTy == cty.String && wantTy == cty.Bool:\n    85→\t\t\t\t\twhat := \"string\"\n    86→\t\t\t\t\tif !val.IsNull() {\n    87→\t\t\t\t\t\twhat = strconv.Quote(val.AsString())\n    88→\t\t\t\t\t}\n    89→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.NilVal, function.NewArgErrorf(0, `cannot convert %s to bool; Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me compile the findings:\n\n## Summary: Sensitive Marks Handling Changes Between v1.9.0 and v1.10.0\n\nBased on my analysis of the Terraform codebase, here are the key changes in how sensitive marks are handled in conditional expressions between v1.9.0 and v1.10.0:\n\n### 1. **HCL Library Upgrade**\n- **v1.9.0**: Uses `github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2 v2.20.0`\n- **v1.10.0**: Uses `github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2 v2.23.0`\n\nThe upgrade to HCL v2.23.0 includes critical fixes for mark propagation in conditional expressions and for-expressions.\n\n### 2. **Critical Bug Fixes in v1.10.0**\n\nThree related issues were fixed:\n\n#### **Issue #36017**: Conditional and for-expression marks loss\n- **Problem**: Evaluation of conditional expressions and for expressions in HCL could lose marks with certain combinations of unknown values\n- **Example scenario**: When a conditional expression evaluated with unknown values, sensitive marks could be lost from the result\n- **Fix**: HCL library now properly combines marks from all branches of conditional expressions\n\n#### **Issue #36012**: IsSensitive function with unknown values\n- **Problem**: The `issensitive()` function could incorrectly assert that an unknown value was not sensitive during plan, but later became sensitive during apply\n- **Root cause**: The function didn't properly handle unknown values - it only checked for the Sensitive mark without considering that unknown values might later become sensitive\n- **Code change in `sensitive.go`**:\n  ```go\n  // v1.9.0\n  Impl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (cty.Value, error) {\n      s := args[0].HasMark(marks.Sensitive)\n      return cty.BoolVal(s), nil\n  }\n  \n  // v1.10.0\n  Impl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (cty.Value, error) {\n      switch v := args[0]; {\n      case v.HasMark(marks.Sensitive):\n          return cty.True, nil\n      case !v.IsKnown():\n          return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Bool), nil  // NEW: Handle unknown values\n      default:\n          return cty.False, nil\n      }\n  }\n  ```\n\n#### **Issue #35501**: Sensitive marks incorrectly transferred to nested resource values\n- **Problem**: Sensitive marks were being transferred to nested values in resources during plan phase, causing erroneous changes\n- **Fix**: Improved mark handling in resource evaluation to prevent unintended mark propagation\n\n### 3. **Sensitive Function Improvements**\n\nThe `sensitive()` function was optimized to preserve existing marks:\n\n```go\n// v1.9.0\nImpl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (ret cty.Value, err error) {\n    val, _ := args[0].Unmark()  // Strips all marks\n    return val.Mark(marks.Sensitive), nil\n}\n\n// v1.10.0\nImpl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (ret cty.Value, err error) {\n    return args[0].Mark(marks.Sensitive), nil  // Preserves existing marks\n}\n```\n\n### 4. **New Ephemeral Mark Support**\n\n**v1.10.0** introduces ephemeral value support:\n\n```go\n// New mark type in marks.go\nconst Ephemeral = valueMark(\"Ephemeral\")\n```\n\n- Includes new `EphemeralAsNullFunc` function for converting ephemeral values to null\n- Handles mark preservation: When removing ephemeral marks, other marks like Sensitive are preserved\n- Properly handles unknown values with ephemeral marks\n\n### 5. **Type Conversion with Unknown Values**\n\nThe `to*` conversion functions were updated to properly handle unknown values with marks:\n\n```go\n// v1.9.0\n// \"AllowUnknown\" was NOT set on the argument parameter\n\n// v1.10.0\nParams: []function.Parameter{\n    {\n        AllowUnknown: true,  // NEW\n        AllowMarked:  true,\n        // ...\n    },\n}\n\nImpl: func(args []cty.Value, retType cty.Type) (cty.Value, error) {\n    if !args[0].IsKnown() {\n        return cty.UnknownVal(retType).WithSameMarks(args[0]), nil  // NEW\n    }\n    // ... conversion logic\n}\n```\n\n### 6. **Conditional Expression Mark Propagation**\n\nThe changelog explicitly states:\n\n> \"In order to ensure consistency in results from HCL conditional expressions, marks must be combined from all values within the expression to avoid losing mark information.\"\n\nThis means:\n- **Before v1.10.0**: Conditional expressions could lose marks when one branch was unknown\n- **After v1.10.0**: Marks are properly combined across all conditional branches\n\n**Example scenario that was fixed**:\n```hcl\n# Before: If condition is unknown, sensitive mark from false_expr could be lost\nresult = var.is_valid ? \"known\" : var.sensitive_value\n\n# After v1.10.0: Sensitive mark is preserved in the result\n```\n\n### 7. **Test Case Improvements**\n\nThe test suite was updated to verify:\n- `issensitive()` returns unknown for unknown values (not just true/false)\n- `sensitive()` preserves additional marks when marking values\n- `ephemeralAsNull()` properly handles mixed ephemeral and sensitive marks\n- Type conversion functions maintain marks when handling unknown values\n\n### 8. **Mark Handling Patterns**\n\n**Correct pattern in v1.10.0**:\n```go\n// Preserve marks when transforming unknown values\nif !value.IsKnown() {\n    return cty.UnknownVal(resultType).WithSameMarks(value), nil\n}\n\n// Or preserve specific marks\n_, marks := value.Unmark()\ndelete(marks, marks.Ephemeral)  // Remove ephemeral\nreturn resultValue.WithMarks(marks), nil\n```\n\n### 9. **Key Takeaway for Users**\n\nUsers may notice that in v1.10.0:\n- **Sensitive values appear more often** - This is correct behavior; marks were being lost incorrectly before\n- **Conditional expressions are more conservative** with marks - If any branch might be sensitive, the result is marked sensitive\n- **The `nonsensitive()` function is more necessary** - To explicitly remove sensitivity marks when intentional\n\nThis is documented in the upgrade notes as a compatibility note where \"users may see sensitive results where they were lost previously.\""},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a6f1d74f71409b04c (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 73758\ntool_uses: 54\nduration_ms: All other valid values of this type are strings\n   585→// containing exactly eight lowercase hex characters.\n   586→type DeposedKey string\n   587→\n   588→// NotDeposed is a special invalid value of DeposedKey that is used to represent\n   589→// the absense of a deposed key, typically when referring to the \"current\" object\n   590→// for a particular resource instance. It must not be used as an actual deposed\n   591→// key.\n   592→const NotDeposed = DeposedKey(\"\")\n   593→\n   594→var deposedKeyRand = rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()))\n   595→\n   596→// NewDeposedKey generates a pseudo-random deposed key. Because of the short\n   597→// length of these keys, uniqueness is not a natural consequence and so the\n   598→// caller should test to see if the generated key is already in use and generate\n   599→// another if so, until a unique key is found.\n   600→func NewDeposedKey() DeposedKey {\n   601→\tv := deposedKeyRand.Uint32()\n   602→\treturn DeposedKey(fmt.Sprintf(\"%08x\", v))\n   603→}\n   604→\n   605→// ParseDeposedKey parses a string that is expected to be a deposed key,\n   606→// returning an error if it doesn't conform to the expected syntax.\n   607→func ParseDeposedKey(raw string) (DeposedKey, error) {\n   608→\tif len(raw) != 8 {\n   609→\t\treturn \"00000000\", fmt.Errorf(\"must be eight hexadecimal digits\")\n   610→\t}\n   611→\tif raw != strings.ToLower(raw) {\n   612→\t\treturn \"00000000\", fmt.Errorf(\"must use lowercase hex digits\")\n   613→\t}\n   614→\t_, err := hex.DecodeString(raw)\n   615→\tif err != nil {\n   616→\t\treturn \"00000000\", fmt.Errorf(\"must be eight hexadecimal digits\")\n   617→\t}\n   618→\treturn DeposedKey(raw), nil\n   619→}\n   620→\n   621→func (k DeposedKey) String() string {\n   622→\treturn string(k)\n   623→}\n   624→\n   625→func (k DeposedKey) GoString() string {\n   626→\tks := string(k)\n   627→\tswitch {\n   628→\tcase ks == \"\":\n   629→\t\treturn \"states.NotDeposed\"\n   630→\tdefault:\n   631→\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"states.DeposedKey(%q)\", ks)\n   632→\t}\n   633→}\n   634→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. *Resources) InstanceValue(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (val cty.Value, live bool) {\n    84→\tr.mu.Lock()\n    85→\tdefer r.mu.Unlock()\n    86→\n    87→\tconfigAddr := addr.ConfigResource()\n    88→\tinsts, ok := r.active.GetOk(configAddr)\n    89→\tif !ok {\n    90→\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, false\n    91→\t}\n    92→\tinst, ok := insts.GetOk(addr)\n    93→\tif !ok {\n    94→\t\t// Here we can assume that if the entire resource exists, the instance\n    95→\t\t// is valid because Close removes resources as a whole. Individual\n    96→\t\t// instances may not actually be present when checks are evaluated,\n    97→\t\t// because they are evaluated from instance nodes that are using \"self\".\n    98→\t\t// The way an instance gets \"self\" is to call GetResource which needs to\n    99→\t\t// compile all instances into a suitable value, so we may be missing\n   100→\t\t// instances which have not yet been opened.\n   101→\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, true\n   102→\t}\n   103→\t// If renewal has failed then we can't assume that the object is still\n   104→\t// live, but we can still return the original value regardless.\n   105→\treturn inst.value, !inst.renewDiags.HasErrors()\n   106→}\n   107→\n   108→// CloseInstances shuts down any live ephemeral resource instances that are\n   109→// associated with the given resource address.\n   110→//\n   111→// This is the \"happy path\" way to shut down ephemeral resource instances,\n   112→// intended to be called during the visit to a graph node that depends on\n   113→// all other nodes that might make use of the instances of this ephemeral\n   114→// resource.\n   115→//\n   116→// The runtime should also eventually call [Resources.Close] once the graph\n   117→// walk is complete, to catch any stragglers that we didn't reach for\n   118→// piecemeal shutdown, e.g. due to errors during the graph walk.\n   119→func (r *Resources) CloseInstances(ctx context.Context, configAddr addrs.ConfigResource) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   120→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   121→\n   122→\t// Use a read-lock here so we can run multiple close calls concurrently for\n   123→\t// different resources. This needs to call CloseEphemeralResource which is sent to\n   124→\t// the provider and can take an unknown amount of time.\n   125→\tr.mu.RLock()\n   126→\tfor _, elem := range r.active.Get(configAddr).Elems {\n   127→\t\tmoreDiags := elem.Value.close(ctx)\n   128→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags.InConfigBody(elem.Value.configBody, elem.Key.String()))\n   129→\t}\n   130→\tr.mu.RUnlock()\n   131→\n   132→\t// Stop tracking the objects we've just closed, so that we know we don't\n   133→\t// still need to close them.\n   134→\tr.mu.Lock()\n   135→\tdefer r.mu.Unlock()\n   136→\tr.active.Remove(configAddr)\n   137→\n   138→\treturn diags\n   139→}\n   140→\n   141→// Close shuts down any ephemeral resource instances that are still running\n   142→// at the time of the call.\n   143→//\n   144→// This is intended to catch any \"stragglers\" that we weren't able to clean\n   145→// up during the graph walk, such as if an error prevents us from reaching\n   146→// the cleanup node.\n   147→func (r *Resources) Close(ctx context.Context) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   148→\t// TODO: Investigate making sure individual close calls are always called\n   149→\t// even after runtime errors. If individual resources should always be\n   150→\t// closed before we get here, then we may not need this at all. If we only\n   151→\t// get here during exceptional circumstances, then we're probably exiting\n   152→\t// anyway so there's no cleanup needed.\n   153→\tr.mu.Lock()\n   154→\tdefer r.mu.Unlock()\n   155→\n   156→\t// We might be closing due to a context cancellation, but we still need to\n   157→\t// be able to make non-canceled Close requests.\n   158→\t//\n   159→\t// TODO: if we're going to ignore the cancellation to ensure that Close is\n   160→\t// always called, should we add some sort of timeout?\n   161→\tctx = context.WithoutCancel(ctx)\n   162→\n   163→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   164→\tfor _, elem := range r.active.Elems {\n   165→\t\tfor _, elem := range elem.Value.Elems {\n   166→\t\t\tmoreDiags := elem.Value.close(ctx)\n   167→\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags.InConfigBody(elem.Value.configBody, elem.Key.String()))\n   168→\t\t}\n   169→\t}\n   170→\tr.active = addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, *resourceInstanceInternal]]()\n   171→\n   172→\t// All renew loops should have returned, or else we're going to leak\n   173→\t// resources which could be continually renewing, or even interfering with\n   174→\t// the same resources during the next operation.\n   175→\t//\n   176→\t// Use an asynchronous check so we can timeout and report the problem.\n   177→\tdone := make(chan int)\n   178→\tgo func() {\n   179→\t\tr.wg.Wait()\n   180→\t\tclose(done)\n   181→\t}()\n   182→\tselect {\n   183→\tcase <-done:\n   184→\t\t// OK!\n   185→\tcase <-time.After(10 * time.Second):\n   186→\t\t// This is probably harmless a lot of time, but is also indicative of an\n   187→\t\t// ephemeral resource which would be misbehaving. The message isn't\n   188→\t\t// very helpful with no context, so we'll have to rely on correlating\n   189→\t\t// the problem via other log messages.\n   190→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(errors.New(\"Ephemeral resources failed to Close during renew operations\"))\n   191→\t}\n   192→\n   193→\treturn diags\n   194→}\n   195→\n   196→type resourceInstanceInternal struct {\n   197→\tvalue      cty.Value\n   198→\tconfigBody hcl.Body\n   199→\timpl       ResourceInstance\n   200→\n   201→\trenewCancel func()\n   202→\trenewDiags  tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   203→\trenewMu     sync.Mutex // hold when accessing renewCancel/renewDiags, and while actually renewing\n   204→}\n   205→\n   206→// close halts this instance's asynchronous renewal loop, if any, and then\n   207→// calls Close on the resource instance's implementation object.\n   208→//\n   209→// The returned diagnostics are contextual diagnostics that should have\n   210→// [tfdiags.Diagnostics.WithConfigBody] called on them before returning to\n   211→// a context-unaware caller.\n   212→func (r *resourceInstanceInternal) close(ctx context.Context) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n   213→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   214→\n   215→\t// if the resource could not be opened, there will not be anything to close either\n   216→\tif r.impl == nil {\n   217→\t\treturn diags\n   218→\t}\n   219→\n   220→\t// Stop renewing, if indeed we are. If we previously saw any errors during\n   221→\t// renewing then they finally get returned here, to be reported along with\n   222→\t// any errors during close.\n   223→\tr.renewMu.Lock()\n   224→\tr.renewCancel()\n   225→\tdiags = diags.Append(r.renewDiags)\n   226→\tr.renewDiags = nil // just to avoid any risk of double-reporting\n   227→\tr.renewMu.Unlock()\n   228→\n   229→\t// FIXME: If renewal failed earlier then it's pretty likely that closing\n   230→\t// would fail too. For now this is assuming that it's the provider's\n   231→\t// own responsibility to remember that it previously failed a renewal\n   232→\t// and to avoid returning redundant errors from close, but perhaps we'll\n   233→\t// revisit that in later work.\n   234→\tdiags = diags.Append(r.impl.Close(ctx))\n   235→\n   236→\treturn diags\n   237→}\n   238→\n   239→func (r *resourceInstanceInternal) handleRenewal(ctx context.Context, wg *sync.WaitGroup, firstRenewal *providers.EphemeralRenew) {\n   240→\tdefer wg.Done()\n   241→\tt := time.NewTimer(time.Until(firstRenewal.RenewAt))\n   242→\tnextRenew := firstRenewal\n   243→\tfor {\n   244→\t\tselect {\n   245→\t\tcase <-t.C:\n   246→\t\t\t// It's time to renew\n   247→\t\t\tr.renewMu.Lock()\n   248→\t\t\tanotherRenew, diags := r.impl.Renew(ctx, *nextRenew)\n   249→\t\t\tr.renewDiags.Append(diags)\n   250→\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   251→\t\t\t\t// If renewal fails then we'll stop trying to renew.\n   252→\t\t\t\tr.renewCancel = noopCancel\n   253→\t\t\t\tr.renewMu.Unlock()\n   254→\t\t\t\treturn\n   255→\t\t\t}\n   256→\t\t\tif anotherRenew == nil {\n   257→\t\t\t\t// If we don't have another round of renew to do then we'll stop.\n   258→\t\t\t\tr.renewCancel = noopCancel\n   259→\t\t\t\tr.renewMu.Unlock()\n   260→\t\t\t\treturn\n   261→\t\t\t}\n   262→\t\t\tnextRenew = anotherRenew\n   263→\t\t\tt.Reset(time.Until(anotherRenew.RenewAt))\n   264→\t\t\tr.renewMu.Unlock()\n   265→\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n   266→\t\t\t// If we're cancelled then we'll halt renewing immediately.\n   267→\t\t\tr.renewMu.Lock()\n   268→\t\t\tt.Stop()\n   269→\t\t\tr.renewCancel = noopCancel\n   270→\t\t\tr.renewDiags = r.renewDiags.Append(ctx.Err())\n   271→\t\t\tr.renewMu.Unlock()\n   272→\t\t\treturn // we don't need to run this loop anymore\n   273→\t\t}\n   274→\t}\n   275→}\n   276→\n   277→func noopCancel() {}\n   278→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. isEphemeral {\n   132→\t\t\t\t// We'll strip the ephemeral mark but retain any other marks\n   133→\t\t\t\t// that might be present on the input.\n   134→\t\t\t\tdelete(givenMarks, marks.Ephemeral)\n   135→\t\t\t\tif !v.IsKnown() {\n   136→\t\t\t\t\t// If the source value is unknown then we must leave it\n   137→\t\t\t\t\t// unknown because its final type might be more precise\n   138→\t\t\t\t\t// than the associated type constraint and returning a\n   139→\t\t\t\t\t// typed null could therefore over-promise on what the\n   140→\t\t\t\t\t// final result type will be.\n   141→\t\t\t\t\t// We're deliberately constructing a fresh unknown value\n   142→\t\t\t\t\t// here, rather than returning the one we were given,\n   143→\t\t\t\t\t// because we need to discard any refinements that the\n   144→\t\t\t\t\t// unknown value might be carrying that definitely won't\n   145→\t\t\t\t\t// be honored when we force the final result to be null.\n   146→\t\t\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(v.Type()).WithMarks(givenMarks), nil\n   147→\t\t\t\t}\n   148→\t\t\t\treturn cty.NullVal(v.Type()).WithMarks(givenMarks), nil\n   149→\t\t\t}\n   150→\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   151→\t\t})\n   152→\t},\n   153→})\n   154→\n   155→func EphemeralAsNull(input cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n   156→\treturn EphemeralAsNullFunc.Call([]cty.Value{input})\n   157→}\n   158→\n   159→// TypeFunc returns an encapsulated value containing its argument's type.
